heart of darkness by joseph conrad contents ii iii the nellie cruise yawl swing to her anchor without flutter of the sail and be at rest
the flood have make the wind be nearly calm and be bind down the river the only thing for it be to come to and wait for the turn of the tide
the sea reach of the thames stretch before we like the beginning of an interminable waterway
in the offing the sea and the sky be weld together without joint and in the luminous space the tan sail of the barge drift up with the tide seem to stand still in red cluster of canvas sharply peak with gleam of varnished sprit
haze rest on the low shore that run out to sea in vanish flatness
the air be dark above gravesend and far back still seem condense into mournful gloom brood motionless over the big and the great town on earth
the director of companies be our captain and our host
we four affectionately watch his back as he stand in the bow look to seaward
on the whole river there be nothing that look half so nautical
he resemble pilot which to seaman be trustworthiness personify
it be difficult to realize his work be not out there in the luminous estuary but behind he within the brood gloom
between we there be as have already say somewhere the bond of the sea
besides hold our heart together through long period of separation it have the effect of make we tolerant of each other 's yarn and even conviction

we feel meditative and fit for nothing but placid staring
the day be end in serenity of still and exquisite brilliance
the water shine pacifically the sky without speck be benign immensity of unstained light the very mist on the essex marsh be like gauzy and radiant fabric hang from the wooded rise inland and drape the low shore in diaphanous fold
only the gloom to the west brood over the upper reach become more sombre every minute as if anger by the approach of the sun
and at last in its curved and imperceptible fall the sun sink low and from glow white change to dull red without ray and without heat as if about to go out suddenly stricken to death by the touch of that gloom brood over crowd of man
forthwith change come over the water and the serenity become less brilliant but more profound
the old river in its broad reach rest unruffled at the decline of day after age of good service do to the race that people its bank spread out in the tranquil dignity of waterway lead to the uttermost end of the earth
we look at the venerable stream not in the vivid flush of short day that come and depart for ever but in the august light of abide memory
and indeed nothing be easy for man who have as the phrase go follow the sea with reverence and affection than to evoke the great spirit of the past upon the low reach of the thames
the tidal current run to and fro in its unceasing service crowd with memory of man and ship it have bear to the rest of home or to the battle of the sea
it have know and serve all the man of whom the nation be proud from sir francis drake to sir john franklin knight all title and untitled the great knight errant of the sea
it have bear all the ship whose name be like jewel flash in the night of time from the golden hind return with her rotund flank full of treasure to be visit by the queen 's highness and thus pass out of the gigantic tale to the erebus and terror bind on other conquest and that never return
it have know the ship and the man
they have sail from deptford from greenwich from erith the adventurer and the settler king ship and the ship of man on change captain admiral the dark interloper of the eastern trade and the commission general of east india fleet
hunter for gold or pursuer of fame they all have go out on that stream bear the sword and often the torch messenger of the might within the land bearer of spark from the sacred fire
what greatness have not float on the ebb of that river into the mystery of an unknown earth
the dream of man the seed of commonwealth the germ of empire

one ship be very much like another and the sea be always the same
in the immutability of their surrounding the foreign shore the foreign face the change immensity of life glide past veil not by sense of mystery but by slightly disdainful ignorance for there be nothing mysterious to seaman unless it be the sea itself which be the mistress of his existence and as inscrutable as destiny

these moribund shape be free as air and nearly as thin
begin to distinguish the gleam of the eye under the tree then glance down see face near my hand

while stand horror strike one of these creature rise to his hand and knee and go off on all fours towards the river to drink
he lap out of his hand then sit up in the sunlight cross his shin in front of he and after time let his woolly head fall on his breastbone

an athletic black belong to some coast tribe and educate by my poor predecessor be the helmsman

there be also the possibility of fall under the suspicion of commercial astuteness deceive the public into the belief that the book have something to do with war
of that however be not afraid very much
what influence my decision most be the obscure prompting of that pagan residuum of awe and wonder which lurk still at the bottom of our old humanity
victory be the last word have write in peace time

such coincidence could not be treat lightly

my object in mention he here be to bring out the fact that far from be the incarnation of recent animosity he be the creature of my old deep seat and as it be impartial conviction
j. c. author 's note on approach the task of write this note for victory the first thing be conscious of be the actual nearness of the book its nearness to personally to the vanish mood in which it be write and to the mixed feeling arouse by the critical notice the book obtain when first publish almost exactly year after the beginning of the war
the writing of it be finish in long before the murder of an austrian archduke sound the first note of warning for world already full of doubt and fear
the contemporaneous very short author 's note which be preserve in this edition bear sufficient witness to the feeling with which consent to the publication of the book
the fact of the book having be publish in the united states early in the year make it difficult to delay its appearance in england any long
it come out in the thirteenth month of the war and my conscience be trouble by the awful incongruity of throw this bit of imagine drama into the welter of reality tragic enough in all conscience but even more cruel than tragic and more inspiring than cruel
it seem awfully presumptuous to think there would be eye to spare for those page in community which in the crash of the big gun and in the din of brave word express the truth of an indomitable faith could not but feel the edge of sharp knife at its throat
the unchanging man of history be wonderfully adaptable both by his power of endurance and in his capacity for detachment
the fact seem to be that the play of his destiny be too great for his fear and too mysterious for his understanding
be the trump of the last judgement to sound suddenly on work day the musician at his piano would go on with his performance of beethoven 's sonata and the cobbler at his stall stick to his last in undisturbed confidence in the virtue of the leather
and with perfect propriety
for what be we to let ourselves be disturb by an angel 's vengeful music too mighty for our ear and too awful for our terror
thus it happen to we to be strike suddenly by the lightning of wrath
the reader will go on read if the book please he and the critic will go on criticize with that faculty of detachment bear perhaps from sense of infinite littleness and which be yet the only faculty that seem to assimilate man to the immortal god
it be only when the catastrophe match the natural obscurity of our fate that even the good representative of the race be liable to lose his detachment
it be very obvious that on the arrival of the gentlemanly mr. jones the single minded ricardo and the faithful pedro heyst the man of universal detachment lose his mental self possession that fine attitude before the universally irremediable which wear the name of stoicism
it be all matter of proportion
there should have be remedy for that sort of thing
and yet there be no remedy
behind this minute instance of life 's hazard heyst see the power of blind destiny
besides heyst in his fine detachment have lose the habit of assert himself
do not mean the courage of self assertion either moral or physical but the mere way of it the trick of the thing the readiness of mind and the turn of the hand that come without reflection and lead the man to excellence in life in art in crime in virtue and for the matter of that even in love
think be the great enemy of perfection
the habit of profound reflection be compel to say be the most pernicious of all the habit form by the civilized man
but would not be suspect even remotely of make fun of axel heyst

j. c. victory an island tale part one chapter one there be as every schoolboy know in this scientific age very close chemical relation between coal and diamond
it be the reason believe why some people allude to coal as black diamond
both these commodity represent wealth but coal be much less portable form of property
there be from that point of view deplorable lack of concentration in coal
now if coal mine could be put into one 's waistcoat pocket but it can not
at the same time there be fascination in coal the supreme commodity of the age in which we be camp like bewildered traveller in garish unrestful hotel
and suppose those two consideration the practical and the mystical prevent heyst axel heyst from go away
the tropical belt coal company go into liquidation
the world of finance be mysterious world in which incredible as the fact may appear evaporation precede liquidation
first the capital evaporate and then the company go into liquidation
these be very unnatural physics but they account for the persistent inertia of heyst at which we out there use to laugh among ourselves but not inimically
an inert body can do no harm to anyone provoke no hostility be scarcely worth derision
it may indeed be in the way sometimes but this could not be say of axel heyst
he be out of everybody 's way as if he be perch on the high peak of the himalayas and in sense as conspicuous
everyone in that part of the world know of he dwell on his little island
an island be but the top of mountain
axel heyst perch on it immovably be surround instead of the imponderable stormy and transparent ocean of air merge into infinity by tepid shallow sea passionless offshoot of the great water which embrace the continent of this globe

part three chapter one tropical nature have be kind to the failure of the commercial enterprise

meditation be always in white man at least more or less an interrogative exercise
heyst meditate in simple term on the mystery of his action and he answer himself with the honest reflection there must be lot of the original adam in after all
he reflect too with the sense of make discovery that this primeval ancestor be not easily suppress

the manager of the tropical belt coal company unpack they on the veranda in the shade besiege by fierce sunshine must have feel like remorseful apostate before these relic
he handle they tenderly and it be perhaps their presence there which attach he to the island when he wake up to the failure of his apostasy

chapter three

chapter ten
the observer be martin ricardo

davidson have be summon to an audience
it be high official on his tour
you know the late baron heyst well
the truth be that nobody out here can boast of having know he well say davidson
he be queer chap
doubt if he himself know how queer he be
but everybody be aware that be keep my eye on he in friendly way
and that be how get the warning which make turn round in my track
in the middle of my trip and steam back to samburan where be grieve to say arrive too late
without enlarge very much davidson explain to the attentive excellency how woman the wife of certain hotel keeper name schomberg have overhear two card sharpe rascal make inquiry from her husband as to the exact position of the island
she catch only few word refer to the neighbouring volcano but there be enough to arouse she suspicions--“which go on davidson she impart to your excellency
they be only too well found
that be very clever of she remark the great man
she be much clever than people have any conception of say davidson
but he refrain from disclose to the excellency the real cause which have sharpen mrs. schomberg 's wit
the poor woman be in mortal terror of the girl be bring back within reach of her infatuated wilhelm
davidson only say that her agitation have impress he but he confess that while go back he begin to have his doubt as to there be anything in it
steam into one of those silly thunderstorm that hang about the volcano and have some trouble in make the island narrate davidson
have to grope my way dead slow into diamond bay
do not suppose that anybody even if look out for could have hear let go the anchor
he admit that he ought to have go ashore at once but everything be perfectly dark and absolutely quiet
he feel ashamed of his impulsiveness
what fool he would have look wake up man in the middle of the night just to ask he if he be all right
and then the girl be there he fear that heyst would look upon his visit as an unwarrantable intrusion
the first intimation he have of there be anything wrong be big white boat adrift with the dead body of very hairy man inside bump against the bow of his steamer
then indeed he lose no time in go ashore alone of course from motif of delicacy
arrive in time to see that poor girl die as have tell your excellency pursue davidson
will not tell you what time have with he afterwards
he talk to
his father seem to have be crank and to have upset his head when he be young
he be queer chap
practically the last word he say to as we come out on the veranda be ah davidson woe to the man whose heart have not learn while young to hope to love and to put its trust in life
as we stand there just before leave he for he say he want to be alone with his dead for time we hear snarly sort of voice near the bush by the shore call out be that you governor
yes it be
think the beggar have do for you
he have start prance and nearly have
have be dodge around look for you ever since
well here be suddenly scream the other voice and then shot ring out this time he have not miss he heyst say to bitterly and go back into the house
return on board as he have insist should do
do not want to intrude on his grief
later about five in the morning some of my calash came run to yell that there be fire ashore
land at once of course
the principal bungalow be blaze
the heat drive we back
the other two house catch one after another like kindling wood
there be no go beyond the shore end of the jetty till the afternoon
davidson sigh placidly
suppose you be certain that baron heyst be dead
he be ash your excellency say davidson wheeze little he and the girl together
suppose he could not stand his thought before her dead body and fire purify everything
that chinaman of whom tell your excellency help to investigate next day when the ember get cool little
we find enough to be sure
he be not bad chinaman
he tell that he have follow heyst and the girl through the forest from pity and partly out of curiosity
he watch the house till he see heyst go out after dinner and ricardo come back alone
while he be dodge there it occur to he that he have well cast the boat adrift for fear those scoundrel should come round by water and bombard the village from the sea with their revolver and winchesters
he judge that they be devil enough for anything
so he walk down the wharf quietly and as he get into the boat to cast she off that hairy man who it seem be doze in she jump up growl and wang shoot he dead
then he shove the boat off as far as he could and go away
there be pause
presently davidson go on in his tranquil manner let heaven look after what have be purify
the wind and rain will take care of the ash
the carcass of that follower secretary or whatever the unclean ruffian call himself leave where it lay to swell and rot in the sun
his principal have shoot he neatly through the head
then apparently this jones go down to the wharf to look for the boat and for the hairy man
suppose he tumble into the water by accident or perhaps not by accident
the boat and the man be go and the scoundrel see himself alone his game clearly up and fairly trap
who know
the water 's very clear there and could see he huddle up on the bottom between two pile like heap of bone in blue silk bag with only the head and the foot stick out
wang be very pleased when he discover he
that make everything safe he say and he go at once over the hill to fetch his alfuro woman back to the hut
davidson take out his handkerchief to wipe the perspiration off his forehead
and then your excellency go away
there be nothing to be do there
clearly assent the excellency davidson thoughtful seem to weigh the matter in his mind and then murmur with placid sadness nothing
october -may lord jim by joseph conrad
author 's note
when this novel first appear in book form notion get about that have be bolt away with
some reviewer maintain that the work start as short story have get beyond the writer 's control
one or two discover internal evidence of the fact which seem to amuse they
they point out the limitation of the narrative form
they argue that no man could have be expect to talk all that time and other man to listen so long
it be not they say very credible
after think it over for something like sixteen year be not so sure about that
man have be know both in the tropic and in the temperate zone to sit up half the night swap yarn
this however be but one yarn yet with interruption afford some measure of relief and in regard to the listener endurance the postulate must be accept that the story be interesting
it be the necessary preliminary assumption
if have not believe that it be interesting could never have begin to write it
as to the mere physical possibility we all know that some speech in parliament have take near six than three hour in delivery whereas all that part of the book which be marlow 's narrative can be read through aloud should say in less than three hour
besides though have keep strictly all such insignificant detail out of the tale we may presume that there must have be refreshment on that night glass of mineral water of some sort to help the narrator on
but seriously the truth of the matter be that my first thought be of short story concern only with the pilgrim ship episode nothing more
and that be legitimate conception
after write few page however become for some reason discontent and lay they aside for time
do not take they out of the drawer till the late mr. william blackwood suggest should give something again to his magazine
it be only then that perceive that the pilgrim ship episode be good starting point for free and wandering tale that it be an event too which could conceivably colour the whole sentiment of existence in simple and sensitive character
but all these preliminary mood and stirring of spirit be rather obscure at the time and they do not appear clear to now after the lapse of so many year
the few page have lay aside be not without their weight in the choice of subject
but the whole be re write deliberately
when sit down to it know it would be long book though do not foresee that it would spread itself over thirteen number of maga
have be ask at time whether this be not the book of mine like well
be great foe to favouritism in public life in private life and even in the delicate relationship of an author to his work
as matter of principle will have no favourite but do not go so far as to feel grieve and annoy by the preference some people give to my lord jim
will not even say that fail to understand
but once have occasion to be puzzled and surprised friend of mine return from italy have talk with lady there who do not like the book
regret that of course but what surprise be the ground of her dislike
you know she say it be all so morbid
the pronouncement give food for an hour 's anxious thought
finally arrive at the conclusion that make due allowance for the subject itself be rather foreign to woman 's normal sensibility the lady could not have be an italian
wonder whether she be european at all
in any case no latin temperament would have perceive anything morbid in the acute consciousness of lose honour
such consciousness may be wrong or it may be right or it may be condemn as artificial and perhaps my jim be not type of wide commonness
but can safely assure my reader that he be not the product of coldly perverted thinking
he be not figure of northern mists either
one sunny morning in the commonplace surrounding of an eastern roadstead see his form pass by appeal significant under cloud perfectly silent
which be as it should be
it be for with all the sympathy of which be capable to seek fit word for his meaning
he be one of we
lord jim chapter
he be an inch perhaps two under six foot powerfully build and he advance straight at you with slight stoop of the shoulder head forward and fix from under stare which make you think of charge bull
his voice be deep loud and his manner display kind of dogged self assertion which have nothing aggressive in it
it seem necessity and it be direct apparently as much at himself as at anybody else
he be spotlessly neat apparelle in immaculate white from shoe to hat and in the various eastern port where he get his living as ship chandler 's water clerk he be very popular
water clerk need not pass an examination in anything under the sun but he must have ability in the abstract and demonstrate it practically
his work consist in race under sail steam or oar against other water clerk for any ship about to anchor greet her captain cheerily force upon he card the business card of the ship chandler and on his first visit on shore pilot he firmly but without ostentation to vast cavern like shop which be full of thing that be eat and drunk on board ship where you can get everything to make she seaworthy and beautiful from set of chain hook for her cable to book of gold leaf for the carving of her stern and where her commander be receive like brother by ship chandler he have never see before
there be cool parlour easy chair bottle cigar write implement copy of harbour regulation and warmth of welcome that melt the salt of three month passage out of seaman 's heart
the connection thus begin be keep up as long as the ship remain in harbour by the daily visit of the water clerk
to the captain he be faithful like friend and attentive like son with the patience of job the unselfish devotion of woman and the jollity of boon companion
later on the bill be send in
it be beautiful and humane occupation
therefore good water clerk be scarce
when water clerk who possess ability in the abstract have also the advantage of having be bring up to the sea he be worth to his employer lot of money and some humour
jim have always good wage and as much humour as would have buy the fidelity of fiend
nevertheless with black ingratitude he would throw up the job suddenly and depart
to his employer the reason he give be obviously inadequate
they say confounded fool
as soon as his back be turn
this be their criticism on his exquisite sensibility
to the white man in the waterside business and to the captain of ship he be just jim nothing more
he have of course another name but he be anxious that it should not be pronounce
his incognito which have as many hole as sieve be not mean to hide personality but fact
when the fact break through the incognito he would leave suddenly the seaport where he happen to be at the time and go to another generally far east
he keep to seaport because he be seaman in exile from the sea and have ability in the abstract which be good for no other work but that of water clerk
he retreat in good order towards the rise sun and the fact follow he casually but inevitably
thus in the course of year he be know successively in bombay in calcutta in rangoon in penang in batavia and in each of these halting place be just jim the water clerk
afterwards when his keen perception of the intolerable drive he away for good from seaport and white man even into the virgin forest the malays of the jungle village where he have elect to conceal his deplorable faculty add word to the monosyllable of his incognito
they call he tuan jim as one might say lord jim
originally he come from parsonage
many commander of fine merchant ship come from these abode of piety and peace
jim 's father possess such certain knowledge of the unknowable as make for the righteousness of people in cottage without disturb the ease of mind of those whom an unerring providence enable to live in mansion
the little church on hill have the mossy greyness of rock see through ragged screen of leaf
it have stand there for century but the tree around probably remember the laying of the first stone
below the red front of the rectory gleam with warm tint in the midst of grass plot flower bed and fir tree with an orchard at the back paved stable yard to the left and the slop glass of greenhouse tack along wall of brick
the living have belong to the family for generation but jim be one of five son and when after course of light holiday literature his vocation for the sea have declare itself he be send at once to training ship for officer of the mercantile marine
he learn there little trigonometry and how to cross top gallant yard
he be generally like
he have the third place in navigation and pull stroke in the first cutter
have steady head with an excellent physique he be very smart aloft
his station be in the fore top and often from there he look down with the contempt of man destine to shine in the midst of danger at the peaceful multitude of roof cut in two by the brown tide of the stream while scatter on the outskirt of the surround plain the factory chimney rise perpendicular against grimy sky each slender like pencil and belch out smoke like volcano
he could see the big ship depart the broad beam ferry constantly on the move the little boat float far below his foot with the hazy splendour of the sea in the distance and the hope of stirring life in the world of adventure
on the low deck in the babel of two hundred voice he would forget himself and beforehand live in his mind the sea life of light literature
he see himself save people from sink ship cut away mast in hurricane swim through surf with line or as lonely castaway barefooted and half naked walk on uncovered reef in search of shellfish to stave off starvation
he confront savage on tropical shore quell mutiny on the high sea and in small boat upon the ocean keep up the heart of despairing man always an example of devotion to duty and as unflinching as hero in book something be up
come along
he leap to his foot
the boy be stream up the ladder
above could be hear great scurrying about and shout and when he get through the hatchway he stand still as if confound
it be the dusk of winter 's day
the gale have freshen since noon stop the traffic on the river and now blow with the strength of hurricane in fitful burst that boom like salvo of great gun fire over the ocean
the rain slanted in sheet that flick and subside and between while jim have threaten glimpse of the tumbling tide the small craft jumble and tossing along the shore the motionless building in the drive mist the broad ferry boat pitch ponderously at anchor the vast landing stage heave up and down and smother in spray
the next gust seem to blow all this away
the air be full of fly water
there be fierce purpose in the gale furious earnestness in the screech of the wind in the brutal tumult of earth and sky that seemed direct at he and make he hold his breath in awe
he stand still
it seem to he he be whirl around
he be jostle
man the cutter
boy rush past he
coaster run in for shelter have crash through schooner at anchor and one of the ship 's instructor have see the accident
mob of boy clamber on the rail cluster round the davit
just ahead of we
mr. symons see it
push make he stagger against the mizzen mast and he catch hold of rope
the old training ship chain to her mooring quiver all over bow gently head to wind and with her scanty rigging hum in deep bass the breathless song of her youth at sea
low away
he see the boat man drop swiftly below the rail and rush after she
he hear splash
let go clear the fall
he lean over
the river alongside seethe in frothy streak
the cutter could be see in the fall darkness under the spell of tide and wind that for moment hold she bind and toss abreast of the ship
yell voice in she reach he faintly keep stroke you young whelp if you want to save anybody
keep stroke
and suddenly she lift high her bow and leap with raise oar over wave break the spell cast upon she by the wind and tide
jim feel his shoulder grip firmly
too late youngster
the captain of the ship lay restrain hand on that boy who seem on the point of leap overboard and jim look up with the pain of conscious defeat in his eye
the captain smile sympathetically
well luck next time
this will teach you to be smart
shrill cheer greet the cutter
she come dance back half full of water and with two exhausted man wash about on her bottom board
the tumult and the menace of wind and sea now appear very contemptible to jim increase the regret of his awe at their inefficient menace
now he know what to think of it
it seem to he he care nothing for the gale
he could affront great peril
he would do so well than anybody
not particle of fear be leave
nevertheless he brood apart that evening while the bowman of the cutter boy with face like girl 's and big grey eye be the hero of the low deck
eager questioner crowd round he
he narrate just see his head bob and dash my boat hook in the water
it catch in his breech and nearly go overboard as think would only old symons let go the tiller and grab my leg the boat nearly swamp
old symons be fine old chap
do not mind bit he be grumpy with we
he swear at all the time he hold my leg but that be only his way of tell to stick to the boat hook
old symons be awfully excitable isn't he
no not the little fair chap the other the big one with beard
when we pull he in he groan oh my leg
oh my leg
and turn up his eye
fancy such big chap faint like girl
would any of you fellow faint for jab with boat hook?--i would not
it go into his leg so far
he show the boat hook which he have carry below for the purpose and produce sensation
no silly
it be not his flesh that hold he his breech do
lot of blood of course
jim think it pitiful display of vanity
the gale have minister to heroism as spurious as its own pretence of terror
he feel angry with the brutal tumult of earth and sky for take he unawares and check unfairly generous readiness for narrow escape
otherwise he be rather glad he have not go into the cutter since low achievement have serve the turn
he have enlarge his knowledge more than those who have do the work
when all man flinch then he feel sure he alone would know how to deal with the spurious menace of wind and sea
he know what to think of it
see dispassionately it seem contemptible
he could detect no trace of emotion in himself and the final effect of staggering event be that unnoticed and apart from the noisy crowd of boy he exult with fresh certitude in his avidity for adventure and in sense of many sided courage
after two year of training he go to sea and enter the region so well known to his imagination find they strangely barren of adventure
he make many voyage
he know the magic monotony of existence between sky and water he have to bear the criticism of man the exaction of the sea and the prosaic severity of the daily task that give bread but whose only reward be in the perfect love of the work
this reward elude he
yet he could not go back because there be nothing more enticing disenchanting and enslaving than the life at sea
besides his prospect be good
he be gentlemanly steady tractable with thorough knowledge of his duty and in time when yet very young he become chief mate of fine ship without ever having be test by those event of the sea that show in the light of day the inner worth of man the edge of his temper and the fibre of his stuff that reveal the quality of his resistance and the secret truth of his pretence not only to other but also to himself
only once in all that time he have again glimpse of the earnestness in the anger of the sea
that truth be not so often make apparent as people might think
there be many shade in the danger of adventure and gale and it be only now and then that there appear on the face of fact sinister violence of intention that indefinable something which force it upon the mind and the heart of man that this complication of accident or these elemental fury be come at he with purpose of malice with strength beyond control with an unbridled cruelty that mean to tear out of he his hope and his fear the pain of his fatigue and his longing for rest which mean to smash to destroy to annihilate all he have see know love enjoy or hat all that be priceless and necessary the sunshine the memory the future which mean to sweep the whole precious world utterly away from his sight by the simple and appalling act of take his life
jim disabled by fall spar at the beginning of week of which his scottish captain use to say afterwards man
it be pairfect meeracle to how she live through it
spend many day stretch on his back dazed batter hopeless and torment as if at the bottom of an abyss of unrest
he do not care what the end would be and in his lucid moment overvalue his indifference
the danger when not see have the imperfect vagueness of human thought
the fear grow shadowy and imagination the enemy of man the father of all terror unstimulated sink to rest in the dullness of exhausted emotion
jim see nothing but the disorder of his toss cabin
he lie there batten down in the midst of small devastation and feel secretly glad he have not to go on deck
but now and again an uncontrollable rush of anguish would grip he bodily make he gasp and writhe under the blanket and then the unintelligent brutality of an existence liable to the agony of such sensation fill he with despairing desire to escape at any cost
then fine weather return and he think no more about it
his lameness however persist and when the ship arrive at an eastern port he have to go to the hospital
his recovery be slow and he be leave behind
there be only two other patient in the white man 's ward the purser of gunboat who have break his leg fall down hatchway and kind of railway contractor from neighbouring province afflict by some mysterious tropical disease who hold the doctor for an ass and indulge in secret debauchery of patent medicine which his tamil servant use to smuggle in with unwearied devotion
they tell each other the story of their life play card little or yawn and in pyjama lounge through the day in easy chair without say word
the hospital stand on hill and gentle breeze enter through the window always fling wide open bring into the bare room the softness of the sky the languor of the earth the bewitching breath of the eastern water
there be perfume in it suggestion of infinite repose the gift of endless dream
jim look every day over the thicket of garden beyond the roof of the town over the frond of palm grow on the shore at that roadstead which be thoroughfare to the east,--at the roadstead dot by garlanded islet light by festal sunshine its ship like toy its brilliant activity resemble holiday pageant with the eternal serenity of the eastern sky overhead and the smile peace of the eastern sea possess the space as far as the horizon
directly he could walk without stick he descend into the town to look for some opportunity to get home
nothing offer just then and while wait he associate naturally with the man of his calling in the port
these be of two kind
some very few and see there but seldom lead mysterious life have preserve an undefaced energy with the temper of buccaneer and the eye of dreamer
they appear to live in crazy maze of plan hope danger enterprise ahead of civilisation in the dark place of the sea and their death be the only event of their fantastic existence that seem to have reasonable certitude of achievement
the majority be man who like himself throw there by some accident have remain as officer of country ship
they have now horror of the home service with its hard condition severer view of duty and the hazard of stormy ocean
they be attuned to the eternal peace of eastern sky and sea
they love short passage good deck chair large native crew and the distinction of be white
they shudder at the thought of hard work and lead precariously easy life always on the verge of dismissal always on the verge of engagement serve chinamen arabs half caste would have serve the devil himself have he make it easy enough
they talk everlastingly of turn of luck how so and so get charge of boat on the coast of china soft thing how this one have an easy billet in japan somewhere and that one be do well in the siamese navy and in all they say in their action in their look in their person could be detect the soft spot the place of decay the determination to lounge safely through existence
to jim that gossip crowd view as seaman seem at first more unsubstantial than so many shadow
but at length he find fascination in the sight of those man in their appearance of do so well on such small allowance of danger and toil
in time beside the original disdain there grow up slowly another sentiment and suddenly give up the idea of go home he take berth as chief mate of the patna
the patna be local steamer as old as the hill lean like greyhound and eat up with rust bad than condemn water tank
she be own by chinaman charter by an arab and command by sort of renegade new south wales german very anxious to curse publicly his native country but who apparently on the strength of bismarck 's victorious policy brutalise all those he be not afraid of and wear blood and iron air combine with purple nose and red moustache
after she have be paint outside and whitewash inside eight hundred pilgrim more or less be drive on board of she as she lie with steam up alongside wooden jetty
they stream aboard over three gangway they stream in urge by faith and the hope of paradise they stream in with continuous tramp and shuffle of bare foot without word murmur or look back and when clear of confine rail spread on all side over the deck flow forward and aft overflow down the yawn hatchway fill the inner recess of the ship like water fill cistern like water flow into crevice and cranny like water rise silently even with the rim
eight hundred man and woman with faith and hope with affection and memory they have collect there come from north and south and from the outskirt of the east after tread the jungle path descend the river coast in praus along the shallow cross in small canoe from island to island pass through suffering meet strange sight beset by strange fear uphold by one desire
they come from solitary hut in the wilderness from populous campong from village by the sea
at the call of an idea they have leave their forest their clearing the protection of their ruler their prosperity their poverty the surrounding of their youth and the graf of their father
they come cover with dust with sweat with grime with rag the strong man at the head of family party the lean old man press forward without hope of return young boy with fearless eye glance curiously shy little girl with tumble long hair the timid woman muffle up and clasp to their breast wrap in loose end of soiled head cloth their sleep baby the unconscious pilgrim of an exacting belief
look at dese cattle say the german skipper to his new chief mate
an arab the leader of that pious voyage come last
he walk slowly aboard handsome and grave in his white gown and large turban
string of servant follow load with his luggage the patna cast off and back away from the wharf
she be head between two small islet cross obliquely the anchoring ground of sailing ship swing through half circle in the shadow of hill then range close to ledge of foam reef
the arab stand up aft recite aloud the prayer of traveller by sea
he invoke the favour of the most high upon that journey implore his blessing on man 's toil and on the secret purpose of their heart the steamer pound in the dusk the calm water of the strait and far astern of the pilgrim ship screw pile lighthouse plant by unbeliever on treacherous shoal seem to wink at she its eye of flame as if in derision of her errand of faith
she clear the strait cross the bay continue on her way through the one degree passage
she hold on straight for the red sea under serene sky under sky scorching and unclouded envelop in fulgor of sunshine that kill all think oppress the heart wither all impulse of strength and energy
and under the sinister splendour of that sky the sea blue and profound remain still without stir without ripple without wrinkle viscous stagnant dead
the patna with slight hiss pass over that plain luminous and smooth unroll black ribbon of smoke across the sky leave behind she on the water white ribbon of foam that vanish at once like the phantom of track draw upon lifeless sea by the phantom of steamer
every morning the sun as if keep pace in his revolution with the progress of the pilgrimage emerge with silent burst of light exactly at the same distance astern of the ship catch up with she at noon pour the concentrated fire of his ray on the pious purpose of the man glide past on his descent and sink mysteriously into the sea evening after evening preserve the same distance ahead of her advance bow
the five white on board live amidships isolate from the human cargo
the awning cover the deck with white roof from stem to stern and faint hum low murmur of sad voice alone reveal the presence of crowd of people upon the great blaze of the ocean
such be the day still hot heavy disappear one by one into the past as if fall into an abyss for ever open in the wake of the ship and the ship lonely under wisp of smoke hold on her steadfast way black and smoulder in luminous immensity as if scorch by flame flick at she from heaven without pity
the night descend on she like benediction chapter
marvellous stillness pervade the world and the star together with the serenity of their ray seem to shed upon the earth the assurance of everlasting security
the young moon recurved and shin low in the west be like slender shaving throw up from bar of gold and the arabian sea smooth and cool to the eye like sheet of ice extend its perfect level to the perfect circle of dark horizon
the propeller turn without check as though its beat have be part of the scheme of safe universe and on each side of the patna two deep fold of water permanent and sombre on the unwrinkled shimmer enclose within their straight and diverge ridge few white swirl of foam burst in low hiss few wavelet few ripple few undulation that leave behind agitate the surface of the sea for an instant after the passage of the ship subside splash gently calm down at last into the circular stillness of water and sky with the black speck of the move hull remain everlastingly in its centre
jim on the bridge be penetrate by the great certitude of unbounded safety and peace that could be read on the silent aspect of nature like the certitude of foster love upon the placid tenderness of mother 's face
below the roof of awning surrender to the wisdom of white man and to their courage trust the power of their unbelief and the iron shell of their fire ship the pilgrim of an exact faith sleep on mat on blanket on bare plank on every deck in all the dark corner wrap in dye cloth muffle in soiled rag with their head rest on small bundle with their face press to bent forearm the man the woman the child the old with the young the decrepit with the lusty all equal before sleep death 's brother
draught of air fan from forward by the speed of the ship pass steadily through the long gloom between the high bulwark sweep over the row of prone body few dim flame in globe lamp be hang short here and there under the ridge pole and in the blurred circle of light throw down and tremble slightly to the unceasing vibration of the ship appear chin upturne two closed eyelid dark hand with silver ring meagre limb drape in torn covering head bend back naked foot throat bar and stretch as if offer itself to the knife
the well to do have make for their family shelter with heavy box and dusty mat the poor repose side by side with all they have on earth tie up in rag under their head the lone old man sleep with draw up leg upon their prayer carpet with their hand over their ear and one elbow on each side of the face father his shoulder up and his knee under his forehead doze dejectedly by boy who sleep on his back with tousled hair and one arm commandingly extend woman cover from head to foot like corpse with piece of white sheeting have naked child in the hollow of each arm the arab 's belonging pile right aft make heavy mound of broken outline with cargo lamp swing above and great confusion of vague form behind gleam of paunchy brass pot the foot rest of deck chair blade of spear the straight scabbard of an old sword lean against heap of pillow the spout of tin coffee pot
the patent log on the taffrail periodically ring single tinkle stroke for every mile traverse on an errand of faith
above the mass of sleeper faint and patient sigh at time float the exhalation of troubled dream and short metallic clang burst out suddenly in the depth of the ship the harsh scrape of shovel the violent slam of furnace door explode brutally as if the man handle the mysterious thing below have their breast full of fierce anger while the slim high hull of the steamer go on evenly ahead without sway of her bare mast cleave continuously the great calm of the water under the inaccessible serenity of the sky
jim pace athwart and his footstep in the vast silence be loud to his own ear as if echo by the watchful star his eye roam about the line of the horizon seem to gaze hungrily into the unattainable and do not see the shadow of the come event
the only shadow on the sea be the shadow of the black smoke pour heavily from the funnel its immense streamer whose end be constantly dissolve in the air
two malays silent and almost motionless steer one on each side of the wheel whose brass rim shine fragmentarily in the oval of light throw out by the binnacle

jim would glance at the compass would glance around the unattainable horizon would stretch himself till his joint crack with leisurely twist of the body in the very excess of well being and as if make audacious by the invincible aspect of the peace he feel he care for nothing that could happen to he to the end of his day
from time to time he glance idly at chart peg out with four drawing pin on low three legged table abaft the steering gear case
the sheet of paper portray the depth of the sea present shiny surface under the light of bull's eye lamp lash to stanchion surface as level and smooth as the glimmering surface of the water
parallel ruler with pair of divider repose on it the ship 's position at last noon be mark with small black cross and the straight pencil line draw firmly as far as perim figure the course of the ship the path of soul towards the holy place the promise of salvation the reward of eternal life while the pencil with its sharp end touch the somali coast lie round and still like naked ship 's spar float in the pool of sheltered dock
how steady she go think jim with wonder with something like gratitude for this high peace of sea and sky
at such time his thought would be full of valorous deed he love these dream and the success of his imaginary achievement
they be the good part of life its secret truth its hidden reality
they have gorgeous virility the charm of vagueness they pass before he with an heroic tread they carry his soul away with they and make it drunk with the divine philtre of an unbounded confidence in itself
there be nothing he could not face
he be so pleased with the idea that he smile keep perfunctorily his eye ahead and when he happen to glance back he see the white streak of the wake draw as straight by the ship 's keel upon the sea as the black line draw by the pencil upon the chart
the ash bucket racket clank up and down the stoke hold ventilator and this tin pot clatter warn he the end of his watch be near

red of face only half awake the left eye partly close the right star stupid and glassy he hang his big head over the chart and scratch his rib sleepily

he pronounce professional remark in voice harsh and dead resemble the rasping sound of wood file on the edge of plank the fold of his double chin hang like bag trice up close under the hinge of his jaw

the thin gold shaving of the moon float slowly downwards have lose itself on the darken surface of the water and the eternity beyond the sky seem to come down near to the earth with the augment glitter of the star with the more profound sombreness in the lustre of the half transparent dome cover the flat disc of an opaque sea
the ship move so smoothly that her onward motion be imperceptible to the sens of man as though she have be crowded planet speeding through the dark space of ether behind the swarm of sun in the appalling and calm solitude await the breath of future creation

say what you like to get its joy to breathe its peace to face its truth one must return with clear conscience
all this may seem to you sheer sentimentalism and indeed very few of we have the will or the capacity to look consciously under the surface of familiar emotion
there be the girl we love the man we look up to the tenderness the friendship the opportunity the pleasure
but the fact remain that you must touch your reward with clean hand lest it turn to dead leaf to thorn in your grasp
think it be the lonely without fireside or an affection they may call their own those who return not to dwelling but to the land itself to meet its disembodied eternal and unchangeable spirit it be those who understand good its severity its saving power the grace of its secular right to our fidelity to our obedience
few of we understand but we all feel it though and say all without exception because those who do not feel do not count

the seventeenth century trader go there for pepper because the passion for pepper seem to burn like flame of love in the breast of dutch and english adventurer about the time of james the first

it make they great

it seem impossible to believe that mere greed could hold man to such steadfastness of purpose to such blind persistence in endeavour and sacrifice
and indeed those who adventure their person and life risk all they have for slender reward
they leave their bone to lie bleach on distant shore so that wealth might flow to the living at home
to we their less try successor they appear magnify not as agent of trade but as instrument of record destiny push out into the unknown in obedience to an inward voice to an impulse beating in the blood to dream of the future

he would kidnap native he would strip some lonely white trader to the very pyjama he stand in and after he have rob the poor devil he would as likely as not invite he to fight duel with shot gun on the beach which would have be fair enough as these thing go if the other man have not be by that time already half dead with fright
brown be latter day buccaneer sorry enough like his more celebrate prototype but what distinguish he from his contemporary brother ruffian like bully hayes or the mellifluous pease or that perfumed dundreary whiskered dandified scoundrel know as dirty dick be the arrogant temper of his misdeed and vehement scorn for mankind at large and for his victim in particular
the other be merely vulgar and greedy brute but he seem move by some complex intention
he would rob man as if only to demonstrate his poor opinion of the creature and he would bring to the shooting or maiming of some quiet unoffending stranger savage and vengeful earnestness fit to terrify the most reckless of desperado
in the day of his great glory he own an armed barque man by mixed crew of kanakas and runaway whaler and boast do not know with what truth of be finance on the quiet by most respectable firm of copra merchant
later on he run off it be report with the wife of missionary very young girl from clapham way who have marry the mild flat footed fellow in moment of enthusiasm and suddenly transplant to melanesia lose her bearing somehow

he be hear of next at nuka hiva where he buy an old french schooner out of government service
what creditable enterprise he might have have in view when he make that purchase can not say but it be evident that what with high commissioners consul man of war and international control the south seas be get too hot to hold gentleman of his kidney
clearly he must have shift the scene of his operation far west because year later he play an incredibly audacious but not very profitable part in serio comic business in manila bay in which peculating governor and an abscond treasurer be the principal figure thereafter he seem to have hang around the philippines in his rotten schooner battle with an adverse fortune till at last run his appoint course he sail into jim 's history blind accomplice of the dark powers

the principal thing be that the cutter throw guard on board make he sail in company towards zamboanga
on the way for some reason or other both vessel have to call at one of these new spanish settlement which never come to anything in the end where there be not only civil official in charge on shore but good stout coasting schooner lie at anchor in the little bay and this craft in every way much well than his own brown make up his mind to steal
he be down on his luck as he tell himself
the world he have bully for twenty year with fierce aggressive disdain have yield he nothing in the way of material advantage except small bag of silver dollar which be conceal in his cabin so that the devil himself could not smell it out
and that be all absolutely all
he be tired of his life and not afraid of death
but this man who would stake his existence on whim with bitter and jeer recklessness stand in mortal fear of imprisonment
he have an unreasoning cold sweat nerve shake blood to water turn sort of horror at the bare possibility of be lock up the sort of terror superstitious man would feel at the thought of be embrace by spectre
therefore the civil official who come on board to make preliminary investigation into the capture investigate arduously all day long and only go ashore after dark muffle up in cloak and take great care not to let brown 's little all clink in its bag
afterwards be man of his word he contrive the very next evening believe to send off the government cutter on some urgent bit of special service
as her commander could not spare prize crew he content himself by take away before he leave all the sail of brown 's schooner to the very last rag and take good care to tow his two boat on to the beach couple of mile off
but in brown 's crew there be solomon islander kidnap in his youth and devoted to brown who be the good man of the whole gang
that fellow swim off to the coaster five hundred yard or so with the end of warp make up of all the run gear unrove for the purpose
the water be smooth and the bay dark like the inside of cow as brown describe it
the solomon islander clamber over the bulwark with the end of the rope in his tooth
the crew of the coaster all tagal be ashore have jollification in the native village
the two shipkeeper leave on board wake up suddenly and see the devil
it have glitter eye and leap quick as lightne about the deck
they fall on their knee paralyse with fear cross themselves and mumbling prayer
with long knife he find in the caboose the solomon islander without interrupt their orison stab first one then the other with the same knife he set to saw patiently at the coir cable till suddenly it part under the blade with splash
then in the silence of the bay he let out cautious shout and brown 's gang who meantime have be peer and strain their hopeful ear in the darkness begin to pull gently at their end of the warp
in less than five minute the two schooner come together with slight shock and creak of spar
brown 's crowd transfer themselves without lose an instant take with they their firearm and large supply of ammunition
they be sixteen in all two runaway blue jacket lanky deserter from yankee man of war couple of simple blond scandinavians mulatto of sort one bland chinaman who cook and the rest of the nondescript spawn of the south seas

he do not give they the time to trans ship enough provision the weather be calm the air be charge with dew and when they cast off the rope and set sail to faint off shore draught there be no flutter in the damp canvas their old schooner seem to detach itself gently from the steal craft and slip away silently together with the black mass of the coast into the night
they get clear away
brown relate to in detail their passage down the straits of macassar
it be harrowing and desperate story
they be short of food and water they board several native craft and get little from each
with steal ship brown do not dare to put into any port of course
he have no money to buy anything no paper to show and no lie plausible enough to get he out again

they slip through unseen or disregard wan sallow face band of utter outcast enrage with hunger and hunt by fear

the shadow go on move
do say voice blank and muffle out of the fog
not one of the many attentive listener understand what the word mean and then brown and his man in their boat float away fade spectrally without the slight sound
thus brown invisible in the mist go out of patusan elbow to elbow with cornelius in the stern sheet of the long boat
perhaps you shall get small bullock say cornelius
oh yes
you will get it if he say so
he always speak the truth
he steal everything have
suppose you like small bullock well than the loot of many house
would advise you to hold your tongue or somebody here may fling you overboard into this damn fog say brown
the boat seem to be stand still nothing could be see not even the river alongside only the water dust fly and trickle condense down their beard and face
it be weird brown tell
every individual man of they feel as though he be adrift alone in boat haunt by an almost imperceptible suspicion of sigh mutter ghost
throw out would you
but would know where be mumble cornelius surlily
have live many year here
not long enough to see through fog like this brown say loll back with his arm swing to and fro on the useless tiller
long enough for that snarl cornelius
that be very useful comment brown
be to believe you could find that backway you speak of blindfold like this
cornelius grunt
be you too tired to row
he ask after silence
no by god shout brown suddenly
out with your oar there
there be great knocking in the fog which after while settle into regular grind of invisible sweep against invisible thole pin
otherwise nothing be change and but for the slight splash of dip blade it be like row balloon car in cloud say brown
thereafter cornelius do not open his lip except to ask querulously for somebody to bale out his canoe which be tow behind the long boat
gradually the fog whiten and become luminous ahead
to the left brown see darkness as though he have be look at the back of the depart night
all at once big bough cover with leaf appear above his head and end of twig dripping and still curve slenderly close alongside
cornelius without word take the tiller from his hand
chapter do not think they speak together again
the boat enter narrow by channel where it be push by the oar blade set into crumble bank and there be gloom as if enormous black wing have be outspread above the mist that fill its depth to the summit of the tree
the branch overhead shower big drop through the gloomy fog
at mutter from cornelius brown order his man to load
will give you chance to get even with they before we be do you dismal cripple you he say to his gang
mind you do not throw it away you hound
low growl answer that speech
cornelius show much fussy concern for the safety of his canoe
meantime tamb itam have reach the end of his journey
the fog have delay he little but he have paddle steadily keep in touch with the south bank
by and by daylight come like glow in ground glass globe
the shore make on each side of the river dark smudge in which one could detect hint of columnar form and shadow of twisted branch high up
the mist be still thick on the water but good watch be be keep for as iamb itam approach the camp the figure of two man emerge out of the white vapour and voice speak to he boisterously
he answer and presently canoe lie alongside and he exchange news with the paddler
all be well
the trouble be over
then the man in the canoe let go their grip on the side of his dig out and incontinently fall out of sight
he pursue his way till he hear voice come to he quietly over the water and see under the now lift swirl mist the glow of many little fire burn on sandy stretch back by lofty thin timber and bush
there again look out be keep for he be challenge
he shout his name as the two last sweep of his paddle run his canoe up on the strand
it be big camp
man crouch in many little knot under subdued murmur of early morning talk
many thin thread of smoke curl slowly on the white mist
little shelter elevate above the ground have be build for the chief
musket be stack in small pyramid and long spear be stick singly into the sand near the fire tamb itam assume an air of importance demand to be lead to dain waris
he find the friend of his white lord lie on raise couch make of bamboo and shelter by sort of shed of stick cover with mat
dain waris be awake and bright fire be burn before his sleeping place which resemble rude shrine
the only son of nakhoda doramin answer his greeting kindly
tamb itam begin by hand he the ring which vouch for the truth of the messenger 's word
dain waris recline on his elbow bid he speak and tell all the news
begin with the consecrate formula the news be good tamb itam deliver jim 's own word
the white man depute with the consent of all the chief be to be allow to pass down the river
in answer to question or two tamb itam then report the proceeding of the last council
dain waris listen attentively to the end toy with the ring which ultimately he slip on the forefinger of his right hand
after hear all he have to say he dismiss tamb itam to have food and rest
order for the return in the afternoon be give immediately
afterwards dain waris lie down again open eyed while his personal attendant be prepare his food at the fire by which tamb itam also sit talk to the man who lounge up to hear the late intelligence from the town
the sun be eat up the mist
good watch be keep upon the reach of the main stream where the boat of the white be expect to appear every moment
it be then that brown take his revenge upon the world which after twenty year of contemptuous and reckless bullying refuse he the tribute of common robber 's success
it be an act of cold blooded ferocity and it console he on his deathbed like memory of an indomitable defiance
stealthily he land his man on the other side of the island opposite to the bugis camp and lead they across
after short but quite silent scuffle cornelius who have try to slink away at the moment of landing resign himself to show the way where the undergrowth be most sparse
brown hold both his skinny hand together behind his back in the grip of one vast fist and now and then impel he forward with fierce push
cornelius remain as mute as fish abject but faithful to his purpose whose accomplishment loom before he dimly
at the edge of the patch of forest brown 's man spread themselves out in cover and wait
the camp be plain from end to end before their eye and no one look their way
nobody even dream that the white man could have any knowledge of the narrow channel at the back of the island
when he judge the moment come brown yell let they have it and fourteen shot ring out like one tamb itam tell the surprise be so great that except for those who fall dead or wound not soul of they move for quite an appreciable time after the first discharge
then man scream and after that scream great yell of amazement and fear go up from all the throat
blind panic drive these man in surge sway mob to and fro along the shore like herd of cattle afraid of the water
some few jump into the river then but most of they do so only after the last discharge
three time brown 's man fire into the ruck brown the only one in view curse and yell aim low
aim low
tamb itam say that as for he he understand at the first volley what have happen
though untouched he fall down and lie as if dead but with his eye open
at the sound of the first shot dain waris recline on the couch jump up and run out upon the open shore just in time to receive bullet in his forehead at the second discharge
tamb itam see he fling his arm wide open before he fall
then he say great fear come upon he not before
the white man retire as they have come unseen
thus brown balance his account with the evil fortune
notice that even in this awful outbreak there be superiority as of man who carry right the abstract thing within the envelope of his common desire
it be not vulgar and treacherous massacre it be lesson retribution demonstration of some obscure and awful attribute of our nature which be afraid be not so very far under the surface as we like to think
afterwards the white depart unseen by tamb itam and seem to vanish from before man 's eye altogether and the schooner too vanish after the manner of steal good
but story be tell of white long boat pick up month later in the indian ocean by cargo steamer
two parched yellow glassy eyed whisper skeleton in her recognise the authority of third who declare that his name be brown
his schooner he report bind south with cargo of java sugar have spring bad leak and sink under his foot
he and his companion be the survivor of crew of six
the two die on board the steamer which rescue they
brown live to be see by and can testify that he have play his part to the last
it seem however that in go away they have neglect to cast off cornelius 's canoe
cornelius himself brown have let go at the beginning of the shooting with kick for parting benediction
tamb itam after arise from amongst the dead see the nazarene run up and down the shore amongst the corps and the expire fire
he utter little cry

afterwards till he have see relate tamb itam he stood look at the heavy canoe and scratch his head
what become of he
tamb itam star hard at make an expressive gesture with his right arm
twice strike tuan he say
when he behold approach he cast himself violently on the ground and make great outcry kick
he screech like frightened hen till he feel the point then he be still and lie star at while his life go out of his eye
this do tamb itam do not tarry
he understand the importance of be the first with the awful news at the fort
there be of course many survivor of dain waris 's party but in the extremity of panic some have swim across the river other have bolt into the bush
the fact be that they do not know really who strike that blow whether more white robber be not come whether they have not already get hold of the whole land
they imagine themselves to be the victim of vast treachery and utterly doomed to destruction
it be say that some small party do not come in till three day afterwards
however few try to make their way back to patusan at once and one of the canoe that be patrol the river that morning be in sight of the camp at the very moment of the attack
it be true that at first the man in she leap overboard and swim to the opposite bank but afterwards they return to their boat and start fearfully up stream
of these tamb itam have an hour 's advance
chapter when tamb itam paddle madly come into the town reach the woman throng the platform before the house be look out for the return of dain waris 's little fleet of boat
the town have festive air here and there man still with spear or gun in their hand could be see move or stand on the shore in group
chinamen 's shop have be open early but the market place be empty and sentry still post at the corner of the fort make out tamb itam and shout to those within
the gate be wide open
tamb itam jump ashore and run in headlong

then he break out very quickly they have kill dain waris and many more
she clap her hand and her first word be shut the gate
most of the fortman have go back to their house but tamb itam hurry on the few who remain for their turn of duty within

next time he go by he answer she think rapidly yes
but we have all the powder in patusan
she catch he by the arm and point at the house call he out she whisper tremble tamb itam run up the step
his master be sleep
it be tamb itam he cry at the door with tiding that can not wait
he see jim turn over on the pillow and open his eye and he burst out at once
this tuan be day of evil an accursed day
his master raise himself on his elbow to listen just as dain waris have do
and then tamb itam begin his tale try to relate the story in order call dain waris panglima and say the panglima then call out to the chief of his own boatman give tamb itam something to eat'”--when his master put his foot to the ground and look at he with such discomposed face that the word remain in his throat
speak out say jim
be he dead
may you live long cry tamb itam
it be most cruel treachery
he run out at the first shot and fall
his master walk to the window and with his fist strike at the shutter
the room be make light and then in steady voice but speak fast he begin to give he order to assemble fleet of boat for immediate pursuit go to this man to the other send messenger and as he talk he sit down on the bed stoop to lace his boot hurriedly and suddenly look up
why do you stand here
he ask very red faced
waste no time
tamb itam do not move
forgive tuan but
but he begin to stammer
what cry his master aloud look terrible lean forward with his hand grip the edge of the bed
it be not safe for thy servant to go out amongst the people say tamb itam after hesitate moment
then jim understand
he have retreat from one world for small matter of an impulsive jump and now the other the work of his own hand have fall in ruin upon his head
it be not safe for his servant to go out amongst his own people
believe that in that very moment he have decide to defy the disaster in the only way it occur to he such disaster could be defy but all know be that without word he come out of his room and sit before the long table at the head of which he be accustomed to regulate the affair of his world proclaim daily the truth that surely live in his heart
the dark power should not rob he twice of his peace
he sit like stone figure
tamb itam deferential hint at preparation for defence
the girl he love come in and speak to he but he make sign with his hand and she be awed by the dumb appeal for silence in it
she go out on the verandah and sit on the threshold as if to guard he with her body from danger outside
what thought pass through his head what memory
who can tell
everything be go and he who have be once unfaithful to his trust have lose again all man 's confidence
it be then believe he try to write to somebody and give it up
loneliness be close on he
people have trust he with their life only for that and yet they could never as he have say never be make to understand he
those without do not hear he make sound
later towards the evening he come to the door and call for tamb itam

there be much weep
