chapter five heavy atmosphere of oppressive quietude pervade the ship
in the afternoon man go about wash clothe and hang they out to dry in the unprosperous breeze with the meditative languor of disenchanted philosopher
very little be say
the problem of life seem too voluminous for the narrow limit of human speech and by common consent it be abandon to the great sea that have from the beginning enfold it in its immense grip to the sea that know all and would in time infallibly unveil to each the wisdom hide in all the error the certitude that lurk in doubt the realm of safety and peace beyond the frontier of sorrow and fear
and in the confused current of impotent thought that set unceasingly this way and that through body of man jimmy bob up upon the surface compelling attention like black buoy chain to the bottom of muddy stream
falsehood triumph
it triumph through doubt through stupidity through pity through sentimentalism
we set ourselves to bolster it up from compassion from recklessness from sense of fun
jimmy 's steadfastness to his untruthful attitude in the face of the inevitable truth have the proportion of colossal enigma of manifestation grand and incomprehensible that at time inspire wonder awe and there be also to many something exquisitely droll in fool he thus to the top of his bent
the latent egoism of tenderness to suffering appear in the develop anxiety not to see he die
his obstinate non recognition of the only certitude whose approach we could watch from day to day be as disquieting as the failure of some law of nature
he be so utterly wrong about himself that one could not but suspect he of have access to some source of supernatural knowledge
he be absurd to the point of inspiration
he be unique and as fascinating as only something inhuman could be he seem to shout his denial already from beyond the awful border
he be become immaterial like an apparition his cheekbone rise the forehead slant more the face be all hollow patch of shade and the fleshless head resemble disinter black skull fit with two restless globe of silver in the socket of eye
he be demoralising
through he we be become highly humanised tender complex excessively decadent we understand the subtlety of his fear sympathise with all his repulsion shrinking evasion delusion as though we have be over civilised and rotten and without any knowledge of the meaning of life
we have the air of be initiate in some infamous mystery we have the profound grimace of conspirator exchange mean glance significant short word
we be inexpressibly vile and very much pleased with ourselves
we lie to he with gravity with emotion with unction as if perform some moral trick with view to an eternal reward
we make chorus of affirmation to his wild assertion as though he have be millionaire politician or reformer and we crowd of ambitious lubber
when we venture to question his statement we do it after the manner of obsequious sycophant to the end that his glory should be augment by the flattery of our dissent
he influence the moral tone of our world as though he have it in his power to distribute honour treasure or pain and he could give we nothing but his contempt
it be immense it seem to grow gradually large as his body day by day shrink little more while we look
it be the only thing about he of he that give the impression of durability and vigour
it live within he with an unquenchable life
it speak through the eternal pout of his black lip it look at we through the impertinent mournfulness of his languid and enormous stare
we watch he intently
he seem unwilling to move as if distrustful of his own solidity
the slight gesture must have disclose to he it could not surely be otherwise his bodily weakness and cause pang of mental suffering
he be chary of movement
he lie stretch out chin on blanket in kind of sly cautious immobility
only his eye roam over face his eye disdainful penetrate and sad
it be at that time that belfast 's devotion and also his pugnacity secured universal respect
he spend every moment of his spare time in jimmy 's cabin
he tend he talk to he be as gentle as woman as tenderly gay as an old philanthropist as sentimentally careful of his nigger as model slave owner
but outside he be irritable explosive as gunpowder sombre suspicious and never more brutal than when most sorrowful
with he it be tear and blow tear for jimmy blow for any one who do not seem to take scrupulously orthodox view of jimmy 's case
we talk about nothing else
the two scandinavians even discuss the situation but it be impossible to know in what spirit because they quarrel in their own language
belfast suspect one of they of irreverence and in this incertitude think that there be no option but to fight they both
they become very much terrify by his truculence and henceforth live amongst we dejected like pair of mute
wamibo never speak intelligibly but he be as smileless as an animal seem to know much less about it all than the cat and consequently be safe
moreover he have belong to the choose band of jimmy 's rescuer and be above suspicion
archie be silent generally but often spend an hour or so talk to jimmy quietly with an air of proprietorship
at any time of the day and often through the night some man could be see sit on jimmy 's box
in the evening between six and eight the cabin be crowd and there be an interested group at the door
every one star at the nigger
he bask in the warmth of our interest
his eye gleam ironically and in weak voice he reproach we with our cowardice
he would say if you fellow have stick out for would be now on deck
we hang our head
yes but if you think be go to let they put in iron just to show you sport
well no
it ruin my health this lie up it do
you do not care
we be as abashed as if it have be true
his superb impudence carry all before it
we would not have dare to revolt
we do not want to really
we want to keep he alive till home to the end of the voyage
singleton as usual hold aloof appear to scorn the insignificant event of an ended life
once only he come along and unexpectedly stop in the doorway
he peer at jimmy in profound silence as if desirous to add that black image to the crowd of shade that people his old memory
we keep very quiet and for long time singleton stand there as though he have come by appointment to call for some one or to see some important event
james wait lay perfectly still and apparently not aware of the gaze scrutinise he with steadiness full of expectation
there be sense of contest in the air
we feel the inward strain of man watch wrestling bout
at last jimmy with perceptible apprehension turn his head on the pillow.—“good evening he say in conciliate tone.—“h'm answer the old seaman grumpily
for moment long he look at jimmy with severe fixity then suddenly go away
it be long time before any one speak in the little cabin though we all breathe more freely as man do after an escape from some dangerous situation
we all know the old man 's idea about jimmy and nobody dare to combat they
they be unsettling they cause pain and what be bad they might have be true for all we know
only once do he condescend to explain they fully but the impression be last
he say that jimmy be the cause of head wind
mortally sick man he maintain linger till the first sight of land and then die and jimmy know that the very first land would draw his life from he
it be so in every ship
do not we know it
he ask we with austere contempt what do we know
what would we doubt next
jimmy 's desire encourage by we and aid by wamibo 's he be finn wasn't he
very well
by wamibo 's spell delay the ship in the open sea
only lubberly fool could not see it
whoever hear of such run of calms and head wind
it be not natural
we could not deny that it be strange
we feel uneasy
the common saying more day more dollar do not give the usual comfort because the store be run short
much have be spoil off the cape and we be on half allowance of biscuit
peas sugar and tea have be finish long ago
salt meat be give out
we have plenty of coffee but very little water to make it with
we take up another hole in our belt and go on scrap polishing paint the ship from morning to night
and soon she look as though she have come out of band box but hunger live on board of she
not dead starvation but steady live hunger that stalk about the deck sleep in the forecastle the tormentor of wake moment the disturber of dream
we look to windward for sign of change
every few hour of night and day we put her round with the hope that she would come up on that tack at last
she do not
she seem to have forget the way home she rush to and fro head northwest head east she run backwards and forwards distract like timid creature at the foot of wall
sometimes as if tired to death she would wallow languidly for day in the smooth swell of an unruffled sea
all up the swing mast the sail thrash furiously through the hot stillness of the calm
we be weary hungry thirsty we commence to believe singleton but with unshaken fidelity dissemble to jimmy
we speak to he with jocose allusiveness like cheerful accomplice in clever plot but we look to the westward over the rail with long eye for sign of hope for sign of fair wind even if its first breath should bring death to our reluctant jimmy
in vain
the universe conspire with james wait
light air from the northward spring up again the sky remain clear and round our weariness the glitter sea touch by the breeze bask voluptuously in the great sunshine as though it have forget our life and trouble
donkin look out for fair wind along with the rest
no one know the venom of his thought now
he be silent and appear thin as if consume slowly by an inward rage at the injustice of man and of fate
he be ignore by all and speak to no one but his hate for every man dwell in his furtive eye
he talk with the cook only having somehow persuade the good man that he donkin be much calumniate and persecute person
together they bewail the immorality of the ship 's company
there could be no great criminal than we who by our lie conspire to send the unprepared soul of poor ignorant black man to everlaste perdition
podmore cook what there be to cook remorsefully and feel all the time that by prepare the food of such sinner he imperille his own salvation
as to the captain he have sail with he for seven year now he say and would not have believe it possible that such man well
there it be can not get out of it
judgment capsize all in minute
strike in all his pride
more like sudden visitation than anything else
donkin perch sullenly on the coal locker swing his leg and concur
he pay in the coin of spurious assent for the privilege to sit in the galley he be dishearten and scandalise he agree with the cook could find no word severe enough to criticise our conduct and when in the heat of reprobation he swear at we podmore who would have like to swear also if it have not be for his principle pretend not to hear
so donkin unrebuked curse enough for two cadge for match borrow tobacco and loaf for hour very much at home before the stove
from there he could hear we on the other side of the bulkhead talk to jimmy
the cook knock the saucepan about slam the oven door mutter prophesie of damnation for all the ship 's company and donkin who do not admit of any hereafter except for purpose of blasphemy listen concentrated and angry gloat fiercely over call up image of infinite torment as man gloat over the accursed image of cruelty and revenge of greed and of power
on clear evening the silent ship under the cold sheen of the dead moon take on false aspect of passionless repose resemble the winter of the earth
under she long band of gold bar the black disc of the sea
footstep echo on her quiet deck
the moonlight cling to she like frosted mist and the white sail stand out in dazzling cone as of stainless snow
in the magnificence of the phantom ray the ship appear pure like vision of ideal beauty illusive like tender dream of serene peace
and nothing in she be real nothing be distinct and solid but the heavy shadow that fill her deck with their unceasing and noiseless stir the shadow dark than the night and more restless than the thought of man
donkin prowl spiteful and alone amongst the shadow think that jimmy too long delay to die
that evening land have be report from aloft and the master while adjust the tube of the long glass have observe with quiet bitterness to mr. baker that after fight our way inch by inch to the western islands there be nothing to expect now but spell of calm
the sky be clear and the barometer high
the light breeze drop with the sun and an enormous stillness forerunner of night without wind descend upon the heated water of the ocean
as long as daylight last the hand collect on the forecastle head watch on the eastern sky the island of flores that rise above the level expanse of the sea with irregular and broken outline like sombre ruin upon vast and deserted plain
it be the first land see for nearly four month
charley be excited and in the midst of general indulgence take liberty with his better
man strangely elated without know why talk in group and point with bared arm
for the first time that voyage jimmy 's sham existence seem for moment forget in the face of solid reality
we have get so far anyhow
belfast discourse quote imaginary example of short homeward run from the islands

jimmy 's death after all come as tremendous surprise
we do not know till then how much faith we have put in his delusion
we have take his chance of life so much at his own valuation that his death like the death of an old belief shake the foundation of our society
common bond be go the strong effective and respectable bond of sentimental lie
all that day we moon at our work with suspicious look and disabuse air
in our heart we think that in the matter of his departure jimmy have act in perverse and unfriendly manner
he do not back we up as shipmate should
in go he take away with himself the gloomy and solemn shadow in which our folly have pose with humane satisfaction as tender arbiter of fate
and now we see it be no such thing

perhaps he be

under white wing she skim low over the blue sea like great tired bird speeding to its nest
the cloud race with her masthead they rise astern enormous and white soar to the zenith fly past and fall down the wide curve of the sky seem to dash headlong into the sea the cloud swift than the ship more free but without home
the coast to welcome she step out of space into the sunshine
the lofty headland tread masterfully into the sea the wide bay smile in the light the shadow of homeless cloud run along the sunny plain leap over valley without check dart up the hill roll down the slope and the sunshine pursue they with patch of run brightness

the channel glitter like blue mantle shoot with gold and star by the silver of the cap sea
the narcissus rush past the headland and the bay
outward bind vessel cross her track lie over and with their mast strip for slogging fight with the hard sou'wester
and inshore string of smoking steamboat waddle hug the coast like migrating and amphibious monster distrustful of the restless wave
at night the headland retreat the bay advance into one unbroken line of gloom
the light of the earth mingle with the light of heaven and above the toss lantern of trawl fleet great lighthouse shine steadily like an enormous rid light burning above vessel of fabulous dimension
below its steady glow the coast stretch away straight and black resemble the high side of an indestructible craft rid motionless upon the immortal and unresting sea
the dark land lie alone in the midst of water like mighty ship bestarre with vigilant light ship carry the burden of million of life ship freight with dross and with jewel with gold and with steel
she tower up immense and strong guard priceless tradition and untold suffering shelter glorious memory and base forgetfulness ignoble virtue and splendid transgression
great ship
for age have the ocean batter in vain her endure side she be there when the world be vast and dark when the sea be great and mysterious and ready to surrender the prize of fame to audacious man
ship mother of fleet and nation
the great flagship of the race strong than the storm
and anchor in the open sea
the narcissus heel over to off shore gust round the south foreland pass through the down and in tow enter the river
shorn of the glory of her white wing she wind obediently after the tug through the maze of invisible channel
as she pass they the red paint light vessel swing at their mooring seem for an instant to sail with great speed in the rush of tide and the next moment be leave hopelessly behind
the big buoy on the tail of bank slip past her side very low and drop in her wake tug at their chain like fierce watchdog
the reach narrow from both side the land approach the ship
she go steadily up the river
on the riverside slope the house appear in group seem to stream down the declivity at run to see her pass and check by the mud of the foreshore crowd on the bank
far on the tall factory chimney appear in insolent band and watch she go by like straggling crowd of slim giant swaggering and upright under the black plummet of smoke cavalierly aslant
she sweep round the bend an impure breeze shriek welcome between her strip spar and the land close in step between the ship and the sea
low cloud hang before she great opalescent and tremulous cloud that seem to rise from the steam brow of million of man
long drift of smoky vapour soil it with livid trail it throb to the beat of million of heart and from it come an immense and lamentable murmur the murmur of million of lip pray curse sigh jeer the undying murmur of folly regret and hope exhale by the crowd of the anxious earth
the narcissus enter the cloud the shadow deepen on all side there be the clang of iron the sound of mighty blow shriek yell

author 's note an outcast of the islands be my second novel in the absolute sense of the word second in conception second in execution second as it be in its essence
there be no hesitation half form plan vague idea or the vague reverie of anything else between it and almayer 's folly
the only doubt suffer from after the publication of almayer 's folly be whether should write another line for print
those day now grow so dim have their poignant moment
neither in my mind nor in my heart have then give up the sea
in truth be cling to it desperately all the more desperately because against my will could not help feel that there be something change in my relation to it
almayer 's folly have be finish and do with
the mood itself be go
but it have leave the memory of an experience that both in thought and emotion be unconnected with the sea and suppose that part of my moral being which be root in consistency be badly shake
be victim of contrary stress which produce state of immobility
give myself up to indolence
since it be impossible for to face both way have elect to face nothing
the discovery of new value in life be very chaotic experience there be tremendous amount of jostle and confusion and momentary feeling of darkness
let my spirit float supine over that chaos
phrase of edward garnett 's be as matter of fact responsible for this book
the first of the friend make for myself by my pen it be but natural that he should be the recipient at that time of my confidence
one evening when we have din together and he have listen to the account of my perplexity fear he must have be grow little tired of they he point out that there be no need to determine my future absolutely
then he add you have the style you have the temperament why not write another
believe that as far as one man may wish to influence another man 's life edward garnett have great desire that should go on writing
at that time and may say ever afterwards he be always very patient and gentle with
what strike most however in the phrase quote above which be offer to in tone of detachment be not its gentleness but its effective wisdom
have he say why not go on writing it be very probable he would have scar away from pen and ink for ever but there be nothing either to frighten one or arouse one 's antagonism in the mere suggestion to write another
and thus dead point in the revolution of my affair be insidiously get over
the word another do it
at about eleven o'clock of nice london night edward and walk along interminable street talk of many thing and remember that on get home sit down and write about half page of an outcast of the islands before sleep
this be commit myself definitely will not say to another life but to another book
there be apparently something in my character which will not allow to abandon for good any piece of work have begin
have lay aside many beginning
have lay they aside with sorrow with disgust with rage with melancholy and even with self contempt but even at the bad have an uneasy consciousness that would have to go back to they
an outcast of the islands belong to those novel of mine that be never lay aside and though it bring the qualification of exotic writer do not think the charge be at all justified
for the life of do not see that there be the slight exotic spirit in the conception or style of that novel
it be certainly the most tropical of my eastern tale
the mere scenery get great hold on as go on perhaps because may just as well confess that
the story itself be never very near my heart
it engage my imagination much more than my affection
as to my feeling for willems it be but the regard one can not help have for one 's own creation
obviously could not be indifferent to man on whose head have bring so much evil simply by imagine he such as he appear in the novel and that too on very slight foundation
the man who suggest willems to be not particularly interesting in himself
my interest be arouse by his dependent position his strange dubious status of mistrust dislike wear out european live on the reluctant toleration of that settlement hide in the heart of the forest land up that sombre stream which our ship be the only white man 's ship to visit
with his hollow clean shave cheek heavy grey moustache and eye without any expression whatever clothe always in spotless sleeping suit much be frog in front which leave his lean neck wholly uncover and with his bare foot in pair of straw slipper he wander silently amongst the house in daylight almost as dumb as an animal and apparently much more homeless
do not know what he do with himself at night
he must have have place hut palm leaf shed some sort of hovel where he keep his razor and his change of sleep suit
an air of futile mystery hang over he something not exactly dark but obviously ugly
the only definite statement could extract from anybody be that it be he who have bring the arabs into the river
that must have happen many year before
but how do he bring they into the river
he could hardly have do it in his arm like lot of kitten
know that almayer found the chronology of all his misfortune on the date of that fateful advent and yet the very first time we din with almayer there be willems sit at table with we in the manner of the skeleton at the feast obviously shun by everybody never address by any one and for all recognition of his existence get now and then from almayer venomous glance which observe with great surprise
in the course of the whole evening he venture one single remark which do not catch because his articulation be imperfect as of man who have forget how to speak
be the only person who seem aware of the sound
willems subside
presently he retire pointedly unnoticed into the forest maybe
its immensity be there within three hundred yard of the verandah ready to swallow up anything
almayer converse with my captain do not stop talk while he glare angrily at the retreat back
do not that fellow bring the arabs into the river
nevertheless willems turn up next morning on almayer 's verandah
from the bridge of the steamer could see plainly these two breakfast together tete tete and suppose in dead silence one with his air of be no long interested in this world and the other raise his eye now and then with intense dislike
it be clear that in those day willems live on almayer 's charity
yet on return two month later to sambir hear that he have go on an expedition up the river in charge of steam launch belong to the arabs to make some discovery or other
on account of the strange reluctance that everyone manifest to talk about willems it be impossible for to get at the right of that transaction
moreover be newcomer the young of the company and suspect not judge quite fit as yet for full confidence
be not much concerned about that exclusion
the faint suggestion of plot and mystery pertain to all matter touch almayer 's affair amuse vastly
almayer be obviously very much affected
believe he miss willems immensely
he wear an air of sinister preoccupation and talk confidentially with my captain
could catch only snatch of mumbled sentence
then one morning as come along the deck to take my place at the breakfast table almayer check himself in his low toned discourse
my captain 's face be perfectly impenetrable
there be moment of profound silence and then as if unable to contain himself almayer burst out in loud vicious tone one thing be certain if he find anything worth have up there they will poison he like dog
disconnected though it be that phrase as food for thought be distinctly worth hear
we leave the river three day afterwards and never return to sambir but whatever happen to the protagonist of my willems nobody can deny that have record for he less squalid fate
j. c. part
an outcast of the island chapter
when he step off the straight and narrow path of his peculiar honesty it be with an inward assertion of unflinching resolve to fall back again into the monotonous but safe stride of virtue as soon as his little excursion into the wayside quagmire have produce the desire effect
it be go to be short episode sentence in bracket so to speak in the flow tale of his life thing of no moment to be do unwillingly yet neatly and to be quickly forget
he imagine that he could go on afterwards look at the sunshine enjoy the shade breathing in the perfume of flower in the small garden before his house
he fancy that nothing would be change that he would be able as heretofore to tyrannize good humouredly over his half caste wife to notice with tender contempt his pale yellow child to patronize loftily his dark skinned brother in law who love pink necktie and wear patent leather boot on his little foot and be so humble before the white husband of the lucky sister
those be the delight of his life and he be unable to conceive that the moral significance of any act of his could interfere with the very nature of thing could dim the light of the sun could destroy the perfume of the flower the submission of his wife the smile of his child the awe strike respect of leonard da souza and of all the da souza family
that family 's admiration be the great luxury of his life
it round and complete his existence in perpetual assurance of unquestionable superiority
he love to breathe the coarse incense they offer before the shrine of the successful white man the man that have do they the honour to marry their daughter sister cousin the rise man sure to climb very high the confidential clerk of hudig co.
they be numerous and an unclean crowd live in ruin bamboo house surround by neglect compound on the outskirt of macassar
he keep they at arm 's length and even far off perhaps have no illusion as to their worth
they be half caste lazy lot and he see they as they be ragged lean unwashed undersized man of various age shuffle about aimlessly in slipper motionless old woman who look like monstrous bag of pink calico stuff with shapeless lump of fat and deposit askew upon decay rattan chair in shady corner of dusty verandah young woman slim and yellow big eyed long haired move languidly amongst the dirt and rubbish of their dwelling as if every step they take be go to be their very last
he hear their shrill quarrelling the squalling of their child the grunting of their pig he smell the odour of the heap of garbage in their courtyard and he be greatly disgusted
but he feed and clothe that shabby multitude those degenerate descendant of portuguese conqueror he be their providence he keep they sing his praise in the midst of their laziness of their dirt of their immense and hopeless squalor and he be greatly delighted
they want much but he could give they all they want without ruin himself
in exchange he have their silent fear their loquacious love their noisy veneration
it be fine thing to be providence and to be tell so on every day of one 's life
it give one feeling of enormously remote superiority and willems revel in it
he do not analyze the state of his mind but probably his great delight lie in the unexpressed but intimate conviction that should he close his hand all those admire human being would starve
his munificence have demoralize they
an easy task
since he descend amongst they and marry joanna they have lose the little aptitude and strength for work they might have have to put forth under the stress of extreme necessity
they live now by the grace of his will
this be power
willems love it
in another and perhaps low plane his day do not want for their less complex but more obvious pleasure
he like the simple game of skill billiard also game not so simple and call for quite another kind of skill poker
he have be the aptest pupil of steady eyed sententious american who have drift mysteriously into macassar from the waste of the pacific and after knock about for time in the eddy of town life have drift out enigmatically into the sunny solitude of the indian ocean
the memory of the californian stranger be perpetuate in the game of poker which become popular in the capital of celebes from that time and in powerful cocktail the recipe for which be transmit in the kwang tung dialect from head boy to head boy of the chinese servant in the sunda hotel even to this day
willems be connoisseur in the drink and an adept at the game
of those accomplishment he be moderately proud
of the confidence repose in he by hudig the master he be boastfully and obtrusively proud
this arise from his great benevolence and from an exalted sense of his duty to himself and the world at large
he experience that irresistible impulse to impart information which be inseparable from gross ignorance
there be always some one thing which the ignorant man know and that thing be the only thing worth know it fill the ignorant man 's universe
willems know all about himself
on the day when with many misgiving he run away from dutch east indiaman in samarang road he have commence that study of himself of his own way of his own ability of those fate compelling quality of his which lead he toward that lucrative position which he now fill
be of modest and diffident nature his success amazed almost frighten he and end as he get over the succeed shock of surprise by make he ferociously conceited
he believe in his genius and in his knowledge of the world
other should know of it also for their own good and for his great glory
all those friendly man who slap he on the back and greet he noisily should have the benefit of his example
for that he must talk
he talk to they conscientiously
in the afternoon he expound his theory of success over the little table dip now and then his moustache in the crushed ice of the cocktail in the evening he would often hold forth cue in hand to young listener across the billiard table
the billiard ball stand still as if listen also under the vivid brilliance of the shaded oil lamp hang low over the cloth while away in the shadow of the big room the chinaman marker would lean wearily against the wall the blank mask of his face look pale under the mahogany marking board his eyelid drop in the drowsy fatigue of late hour and in the buzz monotony of the unintelligible stream of word pour out by the white man
in sudden pause of the talk the game would recommence with sharp click and go on for time in the flow soft whirr and the subdue thud as the ball roll zig zagging towards the inevitably successful cannon
through the big window and the open door the salt dampness of the sea the vague smell of mould and flower from the garden of the hotel drift in and mingle with the odour of lamp oil grow heavy as the night advanced
the player head dive into the light as they bend down for the stroke spring back again smartly into the greenish gloom of broad lamp shade the clock tick methodically the unmoved chinaman continuously repeat the score in lifeless voice like big talk doll and willems would win the game
with remark that it be get late and that he be married man he would say patronizing good night and step out into the long empty street
at that hour its white dust be like dazzling streak of moonlight where the eye seek repose in the dimmer gleam of rare oil lamp
willems walk homeward follow the line of wall overtop by the luxuriant vegetation of the front garden
the house right and leave be hide behind the black mass of flower shrub
willems have the street to himself
he would walk in the middle his shadow glide obsequiously before he
he look down on it complacently
the shadow of successful man
he would be slightly dizzy with the cocktail and with the intoxication of his own glory
as he often tell people he come east fourteen year ago cabin boy
small boy
his shadow must have be very small at that time he think with smile that he be not aware then he have anything even shadow which he dare call his own
and now he be look at the shadow of the confidential clerk of hudig co. go home
how glorious
how good be life for those that be on the win side
he have win the game of life also the game of billiard
he walk fast jingle his winning and thinking of the white stone day that have mark the path of his existence
he think of the trip to lombok for pony that first important transaction confide to he by hudig then he review the more important affair the quiet deal in opium the illegal traffic in gunpowder the great affair of smuggle firearm the difficult business of the rajah of goak
he carry that last through by sheer pluck he have beard the savage old ruler in his council room he have bribe he with gilt glass coach which rumour say be use as hen coop now he have over persuade he he have best he in every way
that be the way to get on
he disapprove of the elementary dishonesty that dip the hand in the cash box but one could evade the law and push the principle of trade to their furth consequence
some call that cheating
those be the fool the weak the contemptible
the wise the strong the respected have no scruple
where there be scruple there can be no power
on that text he preach often to the young man
it be his doctrine and he himself be shin example of its truth
night after night he go home thus after day of toil and pleasure drunk with the sound of his own voice celebrate his own prosperity
on his thirtieth birthday he go home thus
he have spend in good company nice noisy evening and as he walk along the empty street the feeling of his own greatness grow upon he lift he above the white dust of the road and fill he with exultation and regret
he have not do himself justice over there in the hotel he have not talk enough about himself he have not impress his hearer enough
never mind
some other time
now he would go home and make his wife get up and listen to he
why should she not get up?--and mix cocktail for he and listen patiently
just so
she shall
if he want he could make all the da souza family get up
he have only to say word and they would all come and sit silently in their night vestment on the hard cold ground of his compound and listen as long as he wish to go on explain to they from the top of the stair how great and good he be
they would
however his wife would do for to night
his wife
he wince inwardly
dismal woman with startled eye and dolorously droop mouth that would listen to he in pained wonder and mute stillness
she be use to those night discourse now
she have rebel once at the beginning
only once
now while he sprawl in the long chair and drank and talk she would stand at the further end of the table her hand rest on the edge her frightened eye watch his lip without sound without stir hardly breathe till he dismiss she with contemptuous go to bed dummy
she would draw long breath then and trail out of the room relieved but unmoved
nothing could startle she make she scold or make she cry
she do not complain she do not rebel
that first difference of theirs be decisive
too decisive think willems discontentedly
it have frighten the soul out of her body apparently
dismal woman
damn'd business altogether
what the devil do he want to go and saddle himself
he want home and the match seem to please hudig and hudig give he the bungalow that flower bower house to which he be wend his way in the cool moonlight
and he have the worship of the da souza tribe
man of his stamp could carry off anything do anything aspire to anything
in another five year those white people who attend the sunday card party of the governor would accept he half caste wife and all
he see his shadow dart forward and wave hat as big as rum barrel at the end of an arm several yard long
who shout hooray
he smile shamefacedly to himself and push his hand deep into his pocket walk fast with suddenly grave face
behind he to the left cigar end glow in the gateway of mr. vinck 's front yard
lean against one of the brick pillar mr. vinck the cashier of hudig co. smoke the last cheroot of the evening
amongst the shadow of the trim bush mrs. vinck crunch slowly with measured step the gravel of the circular path before the house
there be willems go home on foot and drunk fancy say mr. vinck over his shoulder
see he jump and wave his hat
the crunching of the gravel stop
horrid man say mrs. vinck calmly
have hear he beat his wife
oh no my dear no mutter absently mr. vinck with vague gesture
the aspect of willems as wife beater present to he no interest
how woman do misjudge
if willems want to torture his wife he would have recourse to less primitive method
mr. vinck know willems well and believe he to be very able very smart objectionably so
as he take the last quick draw at the stump of his cheroot mr. vinck reflect that the confidence accord by hudig to willems be open under the circumstance to loyal criticism from hudig 's cashier
he be become dangerous he know too much
he will have to be get rid of say mr. vinck aloud
but mrs. vinck have go in already and after shake his head he throw away his cheroot and follow she slowly
willems walk on homeward weave the splendid web of his future
the road to greatness lie plainly before his eye straight and shin without any obstacle that he could see
he have step off the path of honesty as he understand it but he would soon regain it never to leave it any more
it be very small matter
he would soon put it right again
meantime his duty be not to be find out and he trust in his skill in his luck in his well establish reputation that would disarm suspicion if anybody dare to suspect
but nobody would dare
true he be conscious of slight deterioration
he have appropriate temporarily some of hudig 's money
deplorable necessity
but he judge himself with the indulgence that should be extend to the weakness of genius
he would make reparation and all would be as before nobody would be the loser for it and he would go on unchecked toward the brilliant goal of his ambition
hudig 's partner
before go up the step of his house he stand for awhile his foot well apart chin in hand contemplate mentally hudig 's future partner
glorious occupation
he see he quite safe solid as the hill deep deep as an abyss discreet as the grave
chapter two
the sea perhaps because of its saltness roughen the outside but keep sweet the kernel of its servant soul
the old sea the sea of many year ago whose servant be devote slave and go from youth to age or to sudden grave without need to open the book of life because they could look at eternity reflect on the element that give the life and deal the death
like beautiful and unscrupulous woman the sea of the past be glorious in its smile irresistible in its anger capricious enticing illogical irresponsible thing to love thing to fear
it cast spell it give joy it lull gently into boundless faith then with quick and causeless anger it kill
but its cruelty be redeem by the charm of its inscrutable mystery by the immensity of its promise by the supreme witchery of its possible favour
strong man with childlike heart be faithful to it be content to live by its grace to die by its will
that be the sea before the time when the french mind set the egyptian muscle in motion and produce dismal but profitable ditch
then great pall of smoke send out by countless steam boat be spread over the restless mirror of the infinite
the hand of the engineer tear down the veil of the terrible beauty in order that greedy and faithless landlubber might pocket dividend
the mystery be destroy
like all mystery it live only in the heart of its worshipper
the heart change the man change
the once love and devoted servant go out armed with fire and iron and conquer the fear of their own heart become calculate crowd of cold and exacting master
the sea of the past be an incomparably beautiful mistress with inscrutable face with cruel and promising eye
the sea of to day be use up drudge wrinkled and deface by the churn up wake of brutal propeller rob of the enslave charm of its vastness strip of its beauty of its mystery and of its promise
tom lingard be master lover servant of the sea
the sea take he young fashion he body and soul give he his fierce aspect his loud voice his fearless eye his stupidly guileless heart
generously it give he his absurd faith in himself his universal love of creation his wide indulgence his contemptuous severity his straightforward simplicity of motive and honesty of aim
having make he what he be womanlike the sea serve he humbly and let he bask unharmed in the sunshine of its terribly uncertain favour
tom lingard grow rich on the sea and by the sea
he love it with the ardent affection of lover he make light of it with the assurance of perfect mastery he fear it with the wise fear of brave man and he take liberty with it as spoiled child might do with paternal and good natured ogre
he be grateful to it with the gratitude of an honest heart
his great pride lie in his profound conviction of its faithfulness in the deep sense of his unerring knowledge of its treachery
the little brig flash be the instrument of lingard 's fortune
they come north together both young out of an australian port and after very few year there be not white man in the island from palembang to ternate from ombawa to palawan that do not know captain tom and his lucky craft
he be like for his reckless generosity for his unswerving honesty and at first be little fear on account of his violent temper
very soon however they find he out and the word go round that captain tom 's fury be less dangerous than many man 's smile
he prosper greatly
after his first and successful fight with the sea robber when he rescue as rumour have it the yacht of some big wig from home somewhere down carimata way his great popularity begin
as year go on it grow apace
always visit out of the way place of that part of the world always in search of new market for his cargo not so much for profit as for the pleasure of find they he soon became know to the malays and by his successful recklessness in several encounter with pirate establish the terror of his name
those white man with whom he have business and who naturally be on the look out for his weakness could easily see that it be enough to give he his malay title to flatter he greatly
so when there be anything to be gain by it and sometimes out of pure and unprofitable good nature they would drop the ceremonious captain lingard and address he half seriously as rajah laut the king of the sea
he carry the name bravely on his broad shoulder
he have carry it many year already when the boy willems run barefoote on the deck of the ship kosmopoliet iv
in samarang road look with innocent eye on the strange shore and objurgate his immediate surrounding with blasphemous lip while his childish brain work upon the heroic idea of run away
from the poop of the flash lingard see in the early morning the dutch ship get lumberingly under weigh bind for the eastern port
very late in the evening of the same day he stand on the quay of the landing canal ready to go on board of his brig

chapter three
the opportunity and the temptation be too much for willems and under the pressure of sudden necessity he abuse that trust which be his pride the perpetual sign of his cleverness and load too heavy for he to carry
run of bad luck at card the failure of small speculation undertake on his own account an unexpected demand for money from one or another member of the da souza family and almost before he be well aware of it he be off the path of his peculiar honesty
