under they lingard 's own river ripple softly amongst the pile support the bamboo platform of the little watch house before which they be lie
behind the house the ground rise in gentle swell of low hill clear of the big timber but thickly overgrown with the grass and bush now wither and burn up in the long drought of the dry season
this old rice clearing which have be several year lie fallow be frame on three side by the impenetrable and tangled growth of the untouched forest and on the fourth come down to the muddy river bank
there be not breath of wind on the land or river but high above in the transparent sky little cloud rush past the moon now appear in her diffuse ray with the brilliance of silver now obscure her face with the blackness of ebony
far away in the middle of the river fish would leap now and then with short splash the very loudness of which measure the profundity of the overpower silence that swallow up the sharp sound suddenly
lakamba doze uneasily off but the wakeful babalatchi sit think deeply sigh from time to time and slap himself over his naked torso incessantly in vain endeavour to keep off an occasional and wander mosquito that rise as high as the platform above the swarm of the riverside would settle with ping of triumph on the unexpected victim
the moon pursue her silent and toilsome path attain her high elevation and chase the shadow of the roof eave from lakamba 's face seem to hang arrest over their head
babalatchi revive the fire and wake up his companion who sit up yawn and shiver discontentedly
babalatchi speak again in voice which be like the murmur of brook that run over the stone low monotonous persistent irresistible in its power to wear out and to destroy the hard obstacle
lakamba listen silent but interested
they be malay adventurer ambitious man of that place and time the bohemians of their race
in the early day of the settlement before the ruler patalolo have shake off his allegiance to the sultan of koti lakamba appear in the river with two small trading vessel
he be disappointed to find already some semblance of organization amongst the settler of various race who recognize the unobtrusive sway of old patalolo and he be not politic enough to conceal his disappointment
he declare himself to be man from the east from those part where no white man rule and to be of an oppressed race but of princely family
and truly enough he have all the gift of an exiled prince
he be discontented ungrateful turbulent man full of envy and ready for intrigue with brave word and empty promise for ever on his lip
he be obstinate but his will be make up of short impulse that never last long enough to carry he to the goal of his ambition
receive coldly by the suspicious patalolo he persist permission or no permission in clear the ground on good spot some fourteen mile down the river from sambir and build himself house there which he fortify by high palisade
as he have many follower and seem very reckless the old rajah do not think it prudent at the time to interfere with he by force
once settle he begin to intrigue
the quarrel of patalolo with the sultan of koti be of his fomenting but fail to produce the result he expect because the sultan could not back he up effectively at such great distance
disappoint in that scheme he promptly organize an outbreak of the bugis settler and besiege the old rajah in his stockade with much noisy valour and fair chance of success but lingard then appear on the scene with the armed brig and the old seaman 's hairy forefinger shake menacingly in his face quell his martial ardour
no man care to encounter the rajah laut and lakamba with momentary resignation subside into half cultivator half trader and nurse in his fortified house his wrath and his ambition keep it for use on more propitious occasion
still faithful to his character of prince pretender he would not recognize the constitute authority answer sulkily the rajah 's messenger who claim the tribute for the cultivate field that the rajah have well come and take it himself
by lingard 's advice he be leave alone notwithstanding his rebellious mood and for many day he live undisturbed amongst his wife and retainer cherish that persistent and causeless hope of well time the possession of which seem to be the universal privilege of exile greatness
but the pass day bring no change
the hope grow faint and the hot ambition burn itself out leave only feeble and expire spark amongst heap of dull and tepid ash of indolent acquiescence with the decree of fate till babalatchi fan it again into bright flame
babalatchi have blunder upon the river while in search of safe refuge for his disreputable head
he be vagabond of the sea true orang laut live by rapine and plunder of coast and ship in his prosperous day earn his living by honest and irksome toil when the day of adversity be upon he
so although at time lead the sulu rover he have also serve as serang of country ship and in that wise have visit the distant sea behold the glory of bombay the might of the mascati sultan have even struggle in pious throng for the privilege of touch with his lip the sacred stone of the holy city
he gather experience and wisdom in many land and after attach himself to omar el badavi he affect great piety as become pilgrim although unable to read the inspire word of the prophet
he be brave and bloodthirsty without any affection and he hat the white man who interfere with the manly pursuit of throat cut kidnapping slave dealing and fire raising that be the only possible occupation for true man of the sea
he find favour in the eye of his chief the fearless omar el badavi the leader of brunei rovers whom he follow with unquestione loyalty through the long year of successful depredation
and when that long career of murder robbery and violence receive its first serious check at the hand of white man he stand faithfully by his chief look steadily at the burst shell be undismayed by the flame of the burn stronghold by the death of his companion by the shriek of their woman the wailing of their child by the sudden ruin and destruction of all that he deem indispensable to happy and glorious existence
the beat ground between the house be slippery with blood and the dark mangrove of the muddy creek be full of sigh of the die man who be stricken down before they could see their enemy
they die helplessly for into the tangled forest there be no escape and their swift praus in which they have so often scour the coast and the sea now wedge together in the narrow creek be burn fiercely babalatchi with the clear perception of the come end devote all his energy to save if it be but only one of they
he succeed in time
when the end come in the explosion of the store powder barrel he be ready to look for his chief
he find he half dead and totally blind with nobody near he but his daughter aissa:--the son have fall early in the day as become man of their courage
help by the girl with the steadfast heart babalatchi carry omar on board the light prau and succeed in escaping but with very few companion only
as they haul their craft into the network of dark and silent creek they could hear the cheering of the crew of the man of war 's boat dash to the attack of the rover 's village
aissa sit on the high after deck her father 's blacken and bleed head in her lap look up with fearless eye at babalatchi
they shall find only smoke blood and dead man and woman mad with fear there but nothing else live she say mournfully
babalatchi press with his right hand the deep gash on his shoulder answer sadly they be very strong
when we fight with they we can only die
yet he add menacingly--“some of we still live
some of we still live
for short time he dream of vengeance but his dream be dispel by the cold reception of the sultan of sulu with whom they seek refuge at first and who give they only contemptuous and grudging hospitality
while omar nurse by aissa be recover from his wound babalatchi attend industriously before the exalted presence that have extend to they the hand of protection
for all that when babalatchi speak into the sultan 's ear certain proposal of great and profitable raid that be to sweep the island from ternate to acheen the sultan be very angry
know you you man from the west he exclaim angrily
your word be poison in ruler 's ear
your talk be of fire and murder and booty but on our head fall the vengeance of the blood you drink begone
there be nothing to be do
time be change
so change that when spanish frigate appear before the island and demand be send to the sultan to deliver omar and his companion babalatchi be not surprised to hear that they be go to be make the victim of political expediency
but from that sane appreciation of danger to tame submission be very long step
and then begin omar 's second flight
it begin arm in hand for the little band have to fight in the night on the beach for the possession of the small canoe in which those that survive get away at last
the story of that escape life in the heart of brave man even to this day
they talk of babalatchi and of the strong woman who carry her blind father through the surf under the fire of the warship from the north
the companion of that piratical and son less aeneas be dead now but their ghost wander over the water and the island at night after the manner of ghost and haunt the fire by which sit armed man as be meet for the spirit of fearless warrior who die in battle
there they may hear the story of their own deed of their own courage suffering and death on the lip of live man
that story be tell in many place
on the cool mat in breezy verandah of rajah house it be allude to disdainfully by impassive statesman but amongst armed man that throng the courtyard it be tale which still the murmur of voice and the tinkle of anklet arrest the passage of the siri vessel and fix the eye in absorbed gaze
they talk of the fight of the fearless woman of the wise man of long suffering on the thirsty sea in leaky canoe of those who die
many die
few survive
the chief the woman and another one who become great
there be no hint of incipient greatness in babalatchi 's unostentatious arrival in sambir
he come with omar and aissa in small prau load with green cocoanut and claim the ownership of both vessel and cargo
how it come to pass that babalatchi flee for his life in small canoe manage to end his hazardous journey in vessel full of valuable commodity be one of those secret of the sea that baffle the most search inquiry
in truth nobody inquire much
there be rumour of miss trading prau belong to menado but they be vague and remain mysterious
babalatchi tell story which it must be say in justice to patalolo 's knowledge of the world be not believe
when the rajah venture to state his doubt babalatchi ask he in tone of calm remonstrance whether he could reasonably suppose that two oldish man who have only one eye amongst they and young woman be likely to gain possession of anything whatever by violence
charity be virtue recommend by the prophet
there be charitable people and their hand be open to the deserving
patalolo wag his aged head doubtingly and babalatchi withdraw with shocked mien and put himself forthwith under lakamba 's protection
the two man who complete the prau 's crew follow he into that magnate 's campong
the blind omar with aissa remain under the care of the rajah and the rajah confiscate the cargo
the prau haul up on the mud bank at the junction of the two branch of the pantai rot in the rain warp in the sun fall to piece and gradually vanish into the smoke of household fire of the settlement
only forget plank and rib or two stick neglect in the shiny ooze for long time serve to remind babalatchi during many month that he be stranger in the land
otherwise he feel perfectly at home in lakamba 's establishment where his peculiar position and influence be quickly recognize and soon submit to even by the woman
he have all true vagabond 's pliability to circumstance and adaptiveness to momentary surrounding
in his readiness to learn from experience that contempt for early principle so necessary to true statesman he equal the most successful politician of any age and he have enough persuasiveness and firmness of purpose to acquire complete mastery over lakamba 's vacillate mind where there be nothing stable but an all pervade discontent
he keep the discontent alive he rekindle the expire ambition he moderate the poor exile be not unnatural impatience to attain high and lucrative position
he the man of violence deprecate the use of force for he have clear comprehension of the difficult situation
from the same cause he the hater of white man would to some extent admit the eventual expediency of dutch protection
but nothing should be do in hurry
whatever his master lakamba might think there be no use in poison old patalolo he maintain
it could be do of course but what then
as long as lingard 's influence be paramount as long as almayer lingard 's representative be the only great trader of the settlement it be not worth lakamba 's while even if it have be possible to grasp the rule of the young state
kill almayer and lingard be so difficult and so risky that it might be dismiss as impracticable
what be want be an alliance somebody to set up against the white man 's influence and somebody who while favourable to lakamba would at the same time be person of good standing with the dutch authority
rich and consider trader be want
such person once firmly establish in sambir would help they to oust the old rajah to remove he from power or from life if there be no other way
then it would be time to apply to the orang blanda for flag for recognition of their meritorious service for that protection which would make they safe for ever
the word of rich and loyal trader would mean something with the ruler down in batavia
the first thing to do be to find such an ally and to induce he to settle in sambir
white trader would not do
white man would not fall in with their idea would not be trustworthy
the man they want should be rich unscrupulous have many follower and be well know personality in the island
such man might be find amongst the arab trader
lingard 's jealousy say babalatchi keep all the trader out of the river
some be afraid and some do not know how to get there other ignore the very existence of sambir good many do not think it worth their while to run the risk of lingard 's enmity for the doubtful advantage of trade with comparatively unknown settlement
the great majority be undesirable or untrustworthy
and babalatchi mention regretfully the man he have know in his young day wealthy resolute courageous reckless ready for any enterprise
but why lament the past and speak about the dead
there be one man live great not far off
such be babalatchi 's line of policy lay before his ambitious protector
lakamba assent his only objection be that it be very slow work
in his extreme desire to grasp dollar and power the unintellectual exile be ready to throw himself into the arm of any wander cut throat whose help could be secure and babalatchi experience great difficulty in restrain he from unconsidered violence
it would not do to let it be see that they have any hand in introduce new element into the social and political life of sambir
there be always possibility of failure and in that case lingard 's vengeance would be swift and certain
no risk should be run
they must wait
meantime he pervade the settlement squat in the course of each day by many household fire test the public temper and public opinion and always talk about his impending departure
at night he would often take lakamba 's small canoe and depart silently to pay mysterious visit to his old chief on the other side of the river
omar live in odour of sanctity under the wing of patalolo between the bamboo fence enclose the house of the rajah and the wild forest there be banana plantation and on its further edge stand two little house build on low pile under few precious fruit tree that grow on the bank of clear brook which bubble up behind the house run in its short and rapid course down to the big river
along the brook narrow path lead through the dense second growth of neglect clearing to the banana plantation and to the house in it which the rajah have give for residence to omar
the rajah be greatly impressed by omar 's ostentatious piety by his oracular wisdom by his many misfortune by the solemn fortitude with which he bear his affliction
often the old ruler of sambir would visit informally the blind arab and listen gravely to his talk during the hot hour of an afternoon
in the night babalatchi would call and interrupt omar 's repose unrebuked
aissa stand silently at the door of one of the hut could see the two old friend as they sit very still by the fire in the middle of the beat ground between the two house talk in an indistinct murmur far into the night
she could not hear their word but she watch the two formless shadow curiously
finally babalatchi would rise and take her father by the wrist would lead he back to the house arrange his mat for he and go out quietly
instead of go away babalatchi unconscious of aissa 's eye often sit again by the fire in long and deep meditation
aissa look with respect on that wise and brave man she be accustomed to see at her father 's side as long as she could remember sit alone and thoughtful in the silent night by the die fire his body motionless and his mind wander in the land of memory or who knows?--perhap grope for road in the waste space of the uncertain future
babalatchi note the arrival of willems with alarm at this new accession to the white man 's strength
afterwards he change his opinion
he meet willems one night on the path lead to omar 's house and notice later on with only moderate surprise that the blind arab do not seem to be aware of the new white man 's visit to the neighbourhood of his dwelling
once come unexpectedly in the daytime babalatchi fancy he could see the gleam of white jacket in the bush on the other side of the brook
that day he watch aissa pensively as she move about prepare the evening rice but after awhile he go hurriedly away before sunset refuse omar 's hospitable invitation in the name of allah to share their meal
that same evening he startle lakamba by announce that the time have come at last to make the first move in their long defer game
lakamba ask excitedly for explanation
babalatchi shake his head and point to the flit shadow of move woman and to the vague form of man sit by the evening fire in the courtyard
not word would he speak here he declare
but when the whole household be repose babalatchi and lakamba pass silent amongst sleep group to the riverside and take canoe paddle off stealthily on their way to the dilapidated guard hut in the old rice clearing
there they be safe from all eye and ear and could account if need be for their excursion by the wish to kill deer the spot be well know as the drinking place of all kind of game
in the seclusion of its quiet solitude babalatchi explain his plan to the attentive lakamba
his idea be to make use of willems for the destruction of lingard 's influence
know the white man tuan he say in conclusion
in many land have see they always the slave of their desire always ready to give up their strength and their reason into the hand of some woman
the fate of the believers be write by the hand of the mighty one but they who worship many god be throw into the world with smooth forehead for any woman 's hand to mark their destruction there
let one white man destroy another
the will of the most high be that they should be fool
they know how to keep faith with their enemy but towards each other they know only deception
have see
have see
he stretch himself full length before the fire and close his eye in real or simulate sleep
lakamba not quite convince sit for long time with his gaze rivet on the dull ember
as the night advanced slight white mist rise from the river and the decline moon bow over the top of the forest seem to seek the repose of the earth like wayward and wander lover who return at last to lay his tired and silent head on his beloved 's breast chapter six lend your gun almayer say willems across the table on which smoky lamp shine redly above the disorder of finished meal
have mind to go and look for deer when the moon rise to night
almayer sit sidewise to the table his elbow push amongst the dirty plate his chin on his breast and his leg stretch stiffly out keep his eye steadily on the toe of his grass slipper and laugh abruptly
you might say yes or no instead of make that unpleasant noise remark willems with calm irritation
if believe one word of what you say would answer almayer without change his attitude and speak slowly with pause as if drop his word on the floor
as it be what be the use
you know where the gun be you may take it or leave it
hunt deer
it be
gazelle you be after my honoured guest
you want gold anklet and silk sarong for that game my mighty hunter
and you will not get those for the asking promise you
all day amongst the native
fine help you be to
you should not drink so much almayer say willems disguise his fury under an affected drawl
you have no head
never have as far as can remember in the old day in macassar
you drink too much
drink my own retort almayer lift his head quickly and dart an angry glance at willems
those two specimen of the superior race glare at each other savagely for minute then turn away their head at the same moment as if by previous arrangement and both get up
almayer kick off his slipper and scramble into his hammock which hang between two wooden column of the verandah so as to catch every rare breeze of the dry season and willems after stand irresolutely by the table for short time walk without word down the step of the house and over the courtyard towards the little wooden jetty where several small canoe and couple of big white whale boat be make fast tug at their short painter and bump together in the swift current of the river
he jump into the small canoe balance himself clumsily slip the rattan painter and give an unnecessary and violent shove which nearly send he headlong overboard
by the time he regain his balance the canoe have drift some fifty yard down the river
he kneel in the bottom of his little craft and fight the current with long sweep of the paddle
almayer sit up in his hammock grasp his foot and peer over the river with parted lip till he make out the shadowy form of man and canoe as they struggle past the jetty again
think you would go he shout
will not you take the gun
hey he yell strain his voice
then he fall back in his hammock and laugh to himself feebly till he fall asleep
on the river willems his eye fix intently ahead sweep his paddle right and leave unheede the word that reach he faintly
it be now three month since lingard have land willems in sambir and have depart hurriedly leave he in almayer 's care
the two white man do not get on well together
almayer remember the time when they both serve hudig and when the superior willems treat he with offensive condescension feel great dislike towards his guest
he be also jealous of lingard 's favour
almayer have marry malay girl whom the old seaman have adopt in one of his access of unreasoning benevolence and as the marriage be not happy one from domestic point of view he look to lingard 's fortune for compensation in his matrimonial unhappiness
the appearance of that man who seem to have claim of some sort upon lingard fill he with considerable uneasiness the more so because the old seaman do not choose to acquaint the husband of his adopt daughter with willems history or to confide to he his intention as to that individual 's future fate
suspicious from the first almayer discourage willems attempt to help he in his trading and then when willems draw back he make with characteristic perverseness grievance of his unconcern
from cold civility in their relation the two man drift into silent hostility then into outspoken enmity and both wish ardently for lingard 's return and the end of situation that grow more intolerable from day to day
the time drag slowly
willems watch the succeed sunrise wonder dismally whether before the evening some change would occur in the deadly dullness of his life
he miss the commercial activity of that existence which seem to he far off irreparably lose bury out of sight under the ruin of his past success now go from he beyond the possibility of redemption
he moon disconsolately about almayer 's courtyard watch from afar with uninterested eye the up country canoe discharge guttah or rattan and load rice or european good on the little wharf of lingard co.
big as be the extent of ground own by almayer willems yet feel that there be not enough room for he inside those neat fence
the man who during long year become accustomed to think of himself as indispensable to other feel bitter and savage rage at the cruel consciousness of his superfluity of his uselessness at the cold hostility visible in every look of the only white man in this barbarous corner of the world
he gnash his tooth when he think of the wasted day of the life throw away in the unwilling company of that peevish and suspicious fool
he hear the reproach of his idleness in the murmur of the river in the unceasing whisper of the great forest
round he everything stir move sweep by in rush the earth under his foot and the heaven above his head
the very savage around he strove struggle fight work if only to prolong miserable existence but they live they live
and it be only himself that seem to be leave outside the scheme of creation in hopeless immobility fill with torment anger and with ever stinging regret
he take to wander about the settlement
the afterwards flourish sambir be bear in swamp and pass its youth in malodorous mud
the house crowd the bank and as if to get away from the unhealthy shore step boldly into the river shoot over it in close row of bamboo platform elevate on high pile amongst which the current below speak in soft and unceasing plaint of murmur eddy
there be only one path in the whole town and it run at the back of the house along the succession of blacken circular patch that mark the place of the household fire
on the other side the virgin forest border the path come close to it as if to provoke impudently any passer by to the solution of the gloomy problem of its depth
nobody would accept the deceptive challenge
there be only few feeble attempt at clearing here and there but the ground be low and the river retire after its yearly flood leave on each gradually diminish mudhole where the import buffalo of the bugis settler wallow happily during the heat of the day
when willems walk on the path the indolent man stretch on the shady side of the house look at he with calm curiosity the woman busy round the cooking fire would send after he wonder and timid glance while the child would only look once and then run away yell with fright at the horrible appearance of the man with red and white face
these manifestation of childish disgust and fear sting willems with sense of absurd humiliation he seek in his walk the comparative solitude of the rudimentary clearing but the very buffalo snort with alarm at his sight scramble lumberingly out of the cool mud and star wildly in compact herd at he as he try to slink unperceive along the edge of the forest
one day at some unguarded and sudden movement of his the whole herd stampede down the path scatter the fire send the woman fly with shrill cry and leave behind track of smash pot trample rice overturn child and crowd of angry man brandish stick in loud voice pursuit
the innocent cause of that disturbance run shamefacedly the gauntlet of black look and unfriendly remark and hastily seek refuge in almayer 's campong
after that he leave the settlement alone
later when the enforce confinement grow irksome willems take one of almayer 's many canoe and cross the main branch of the pantai in search of some solitary spot where he could hide his discouragement and his weariness
he skirt in his little craft the wall of tangle verdure keep in the dead water close to the bank where the spread nipa palm nod their broad leaf over his head as if in contemptuous pity of the wander outcast
here and there he could see the beginning of chop out pathway and with the fix idea of get out of sight of the busy river he would land and follow the narrow and winding path only to find that it lead nowhere end abruptly in the discouragement of thorny thicket
he would go back slowly with bitter sense of unreasonable disappointment and sadness oppress by the hot smell of earth dampness and decay in that forest which seem to push he mercilessly back into the glitter sunshine of the river
and he would recommence paddle with tired arm to seek another opening to find another deception
as he paddle up to the point where the rajah 's stockade come down to the river the nipas be leave behind rattle their leaf over the brown water and the big tree would appear on the bank tall strong indifferent in the immense solidity of their life which endure for age to that short and fleeting life in the heart of the man who creep painfully amongst their shadow in search of refuge from the unceasing reproach of his thought
amongst their smooth trunk clear brook meander for time in twin lacet before it make up its mind to take leap into the hurrying river over the edge of the steep bank
there be also pathway there and it seem frequent
willems land and follow the capricious promise of the track soon find himself in comparatively clear space where the confused tracery of sunlight fall through the branch and the foliage overhead and lie on the stream that shine in an easy curve like bright sword blade drop amongst the long and feathery grass
far on the path continue narrow again in the thick undergrowth
at the end of the first turn willems see flash of white and colour gleam of gold like sun ray lose in shadow and vision of blackness dark than the deep shade of the forest
he stop surprise and fancy he have hear light footstep grow light cease
he look around
the grass on the bank of the stream tremble and tremulous path of its shivering silver grey top run from the water to the beginning of the thicket
and yet there be not breath of wind
somebody kind pass there
he look pensive while the tremor die out in quick tremble under his eye and the grass stand high unstirre with droop head in the warm and motionless air
he hurry on drive by suddenly awaken curiosity and enter the narrow way between the bush
at the next turn of the path he catch again the glimpse of coloured stuff and of woman 's black hair before he
he hasten his pace and come in full view of the object of his pursuit
the woman who be carry two bamboo vessel full of water hear his footstep stop and put the bamboo down half turn to look back
willems also stand still for minute then walk steadily on with firm tread while the woman move aside to let he pass
he keep his eye fix straight before he yet almost unconsciously he take in every detail of the tall and graceful figure
as he approach she the woman toss her head slightly back and with free gesture of her strong round arm catch up the mass of loose black hair and bring it over her shoulder and across the low part of her face
the next moment he be pass she close walk rigidly like man in trance
he hear her rapid breathing and he feel the touch of look dart at he from half open eye
it touch his brain and his heart together
it seem to he to be something loud and stir like shout silent and penetrate like an inspiration
the momentum of his motion carry he past she but an invisible force make up of surprise and curiosity and desire spin he round as soon as he have pass
she have take up her burden already with the intention of pursue her path
his sudden movement arrest she at the first step and again she stand straight slim expectant with readiness to dart away suggest in the light immobility of her pose
high above the branch of the tree meet in transparent shimmer of wave green mist through which the rain of yellow ray descend upon her head stream in glint down her black tress shine with the change glow of liquid metal on her face and lose itself in vanish spark in the sombre depth of her eye that wide open now with enlarged pupil look steadily at the man in her path
and willems star at she charm with charm that carry with it sense of irreparable loss tingle with that feeling which begin like caress and end in blow in that sudden hurt of new emotion make its way into human heart with the brusque stirring of sleep sensation awaken suddenly to the rush of new hope new fear new desire and to the flight of one 's old self
she move step forward and again halt
breath of wind that come through the tree but in willems fancy seem to be drive by her move figure ripple in hot wave round his body and scorch his face in burn touch
he draw it in with long breath the last long breath of soldier before the rush of battle of lover before he take in his arm the adore woman the breath that give courage to confront the menace of death or the storm of passion
who be she
where do she come from
wonderingly he take his eye off her face to look round at the serried tree of the forest that stand big and still and straight as if watch he and she breathlessly
he have be baffle repel almost frightened by the intensity of that tropical life which want the sunshine but work in gloom which seem to be all grace of colour and form all brilliance all smile but be only the blossoming of the dead whose mystery hold the promise of joy and beauty yet contain nothing but poison and decay
he have be frighten by the vague perception of danger before but now as he look at that life again his eye seem able to pierce the fantastic veil of creeper and leaf to look past the solid trunk to see through the forbid gloom and the mystery be disclose enchanting subduing beautiful
he look at the woman
through the checkered light between they she appear to he with the impalpable distinctness of dream
the very spirit of that land of mysterious forest stand before he like an apparition behind transparent veil veil weave of sunbeam and shadow
she have approach he still near
he feel strange impatience within he at her advance
confused thought rush through his head disorder shapeless stunning
then he hear his own voice asking-- who be you
be the daughter of the blind omar she answer in low but steady tone
and you she go on little louder you be the white trader the great man of this place
yes say willems hold her eye with his in sense of extreme effort yes be white
then he add feel as if he speak about some other man but be the outcast of my people
she listen to he gravely
through the mesh of scatter hair her face look like the face of golden statue with live eye
the heavy eyelid drop slightly and from between the long eyelash she send out sidelong look hard keen and narrow like the gleam of sharp steel
her lip be firm and compose in graceful curve but the distend nostril the upward poise of the half avert head give to her whole person the expression of wild and resentful defiance
shadow pass over willems face
he put his hand over his lip as if to keep back the word that want to come out in surge of impulsive necessity the outcome of dominant think that rush from the heart to the brain and must be speak in the face of doubt of danger of fear of destruction itself
you be beautiful he whisper
she look at he again with glance that run in one quick flash of her eye over his sunburnt feature his broad shoulder his straight tall motionless figure rest at last on the ground at his foot
then she smile
in the sombre beauty of her face that smile be like the first ray of light on stormy daybreak that dart evanescent and pale through the gloomy cloud the forerunner of sunrise and of thunder
chapter seven
there be in our life short period which hold no place in memory but only as the recollection of feeling
there be no remembrance of gesture of action of any outward manifestation of life those be lose in the unearthly brilliance or in the unearthly gloom of such moment
we be absorb in the contemplation of that something within our body which rejoice or suffer while the body go on breathing instinctively run away or not less instinctively fight perhaps die
but death in such moment be the privilege of the fortunate it be high and rare favour supreme grace
willems never remember how and when he part from aissa
he catch himself drink the muddy water out of the hollow of his hand while his canoe be drift in mid stream past the last house of sambir
with his return wit come the fear of something unknown that have take possession of his heart of something inarticulate and masterful which could not speak and would be obey
his first impulse be that of revolt
he would never go back there
he look round slowly at the brilliance of thing in the deadly sunshine and take up his paddle
how change everything seem
the river be broad the sky be high
how fast the canoe fly under the stroke of his paddle
since when have he acquire the strength of two man or more
he look up and down the reach at the forest of the bank with confused notion that with one sweep of his hand he could tumble all these tree into the stream
his face felt burn
he drink again and shudder with depraved sense of pleasure at the after taste of slime in the water
it be late when he reach almayer 's house but he cross the dark and uneven courtyard walk lightly in the radiance of some light of his own invisible to other eye
his host 's sulky greeting jar he like sudden fall down great height
he take his place at the table opposite almayer and try to speak cheerfully to his gloomy companion but when the meal be end and they sit smoke in silence he feel an abrupt discouragement lassitude in all his limb sense of immense sadness as after some great and irreparable loss
the darkness of the night enter his heart bring with it doubt and hesitation and dull anger with himself and all the world
he have an impulse to shout horrible curse to quarrel with almayer to do something violent
quite without any immediate provocation he think he would like to assault the wretched sulky beast
he glance at he ferociously from under his eyebrow
the unconscious almayer smoke thoughtfully plan to morrow 's work probably
the man 's composure seem to willems an unpardonable insult
why do not that idiot talk to night when he want he to
on other night he be ready enough to chatter
and such dull nonsense too
and willems try hard to repress his own senseless rage look fixedly through the thick tobacco smoke at the stain tablecloth
they retire early as usual but in the middle of the night willems leap out of his hammock with stifle execration and run down the step into the courtyard
the two night watchman who sit by little fire talk together in monotonous undertone lift their head to look wonderingly at the discomposed feature of the white man as he cross the circle of light throw out by their fire
he disappear in the darkness and then come back again pass they close but with no sign of consciousness of their presence on his face
backwards and forwards he pace mutter to himself and the two malays after short consultation in whisper leave the fire quietly not think it safe to remain in the vicinity of white man who behave in such strange manner
they retire round the corner of the godown and watch willems curiously through the night till the short daybreak be follow by the sudden blaze of the rise sun and almayer 's establishment wake up to life and work
as soon as he could get away unnoticed in the bustle of the busy riverside willems cross the river on his way to the place where he have meet aissa
he throw himself down in the grass by the side of the brook and listen for the sound of her footstep
the brilliant light of day fall through the irregular opening in the high branch of the tree and stream down soften amongst the shadow of big trunk
here and there narrow sunbeam touch the rugged bark of tree with golden splash sparkle on the leap water of the brook or rest on leaf that stand out shimmering and distinct on the monotonous background of sombre green tint
the clear gap of blue above his head be cross by the quick flight of white rice bird whose wing flash in the sunlight while through it the heat pour down from the sky cling about the steam earth roll among the tree and wrap up willems in the soft and odorous fold of air heavy with the faint scent of blossom and with the acrid smell of decay life
and in that atmosphere of nature 's workshop willems felt soothe and lull into forgetfulness of his past into indifference as to his future
the recollection of his triumph of his wrong and of his ambition vanish in that warmth which seem to melt all regret all hope all anger all strength out of his heart
and he lie there dreamily contented in the tepid and perfumed shelter thinking of aissa 's eye recall the sound of her voice the quiver of her lip her frown and her smile
she come of course
to she he be something new unknown and strange
he be big strong than any man she have see before and altogether different from all those she know
he be of the victorious race
with vivid remembrance of the great catastrophe of her life he appear to she with all the fascination of great and dangerous thing of terror vanquish surmount make plaything of
they speak with just such deep voice those victorious man they look with just such hard blue eye at their enemy
and she make that voice speak softly to she those eye look tenderly at her face
he be indeed man
she could not understand all he tell she of his life but the fragment she understand she make up for herself into story of man great amongst his own people valorous and unfortunate an undaunted fugitive dreaming of vengeance against his enemy
he have all the attractiveness of the vague and the unknown of the unforeseen and of the sudden of be strong dangerous alive and human ready to be enslave
she feel that he be ready
she feel it with the unerring intuition of primitive woman confront by simple impulse
day after day when they meet and she stand little way off listen to his word hold he with her look the undefined terror of the new conquest become faint and blur like the memory of dream and the certitude grow distinct and convincing and visible to the eye like some material thing in full sunlight
it be deep joy great pride tangible sweetness that seem to leave the taste of honey on her lip
he lie stretch at her foot without move for he know from experience how slight movement of his could frighten she away in those first day of their intercourse
he lie very quiet with all the ardour of his desire ring in his voice and shin in his eye whilst his body be still like death itself
and he look at she stand above he her head lose in the shadow of broad and graceful leaf that touch her cheek while the slender spike of pale green orchid stream down from amongst the bough and mingle with the black hair that frame her face as if all those plant claim she for their own the animated and brilliant flower of all that exuberant life which bear in gloom struggle for ever towards the sunshine
every day she come little near
he watch her slow progress the gradual taming of that woman by the word of his love
it be the monotonous song of praise and desire that commence at creation wrap up the world like an atmosphere and shall end only in the end of all thing when there be no lip to sing and no ear to hear
he tell she that she be beautiful and desirable and he repeat it again and again for when he tell she that he have say all there be within he he have express his only thought his only feeling
and he watch the startled look of wonder and mistrust vanish from her face with the passing day her eye soften the smile dwell long and long on her lip smile as of one charm by delightful dream with the slight exaltation of intoxicating triumph lurk in its dawn tenderness
and while she be near there be nothing in the whole world for that idle man but her look and her smile
nothing in the past nothing in the future and in the present only the luminous fact of her existence
but in the sudden darkness of she go he would be leave weak and helpless as though despoil violently of all that be himself
he who have live all his life with no preoccupation but that of his own career contemptuously indifferent to all feminine influence full of scorn for man that would submit to it if ever so little he so strong so superior even in his error realize at last that his very individuality be snatch from within himself by the hand of woman
where be the assurance and pride of his cleverness the belief in success the anger of failure the wish to retrieve his fortune the certitude of his ability to accomplish it yet
all go
all that have be man within he be go and there remain only the trouble of his heart that heart which have become contemptible thing which could be flutter by look or smile torment by word soothe by promise
when the long for day come at last when she sink on the grass by his side and with quick gesture take his hand in her he sit up suddenly with the movement and look of man awaken by the crash of his own fall house
all his blood all his sensation all his life seem to rush into that hand leave he without strength in cold shiver in the sudden clamminess and collapse as of deadly gun shot wound
he fling her hand away brutally like something burn and sit motionless his head fall forward star on the ground and catch his breath in painful gasps
his impulse of fear and apparent horror do not dismay she in the least
her face be grave and her eye look seriously at he
her finger touch the hair of his temple run in light caress down his cheek twist gently the end of his long moustache and while he sit in the tremor of that contact she run off with startling fleetness and disappear in peal of clear laughter in the stir of grass in the nod of young twig grow over the path leave behind only vanish trail of motion and sound
he scramble to his foot slowly and painfully like man with burden on his shoulder and walk towards the riverside
he hug to his breast the recollection of his fear and of his delight but tell himself seriously over and over again that this must be the end of that adventure
after shove off his canoe into the stream he lift his eye to the bank and gaze at it long and steadily as if take his last look at place of charming memory
he march up to almayer 's house with the concentrated expression and the determined step of man who have just take momentous resolution
his face be set and rigid his gesture and movement be guard and slow
he be keep tight hand on himself
very tight hand
he have vivid illusion as vivid as reality almost of be in charge of slippery prisoner
he sit opposite almayer during that dinner which be their last meal together with perfectly calm face and within he grow terror of escape from his own self
now and then he would grasp the edge of the table and set his tooth hard in sudden wave of acute despair like one who fall down smooth and rapid declivity that end in precipice dig his finger nail into the yield surface and feel himself slip helplessly to inevitable destruction
then abruptly come relaxation of his muscle the give way of his will
something seem to snap in his head and that wish that idea keep back during all those hour dart into his brain with the heat and noise of conflagration
he must see she
see she at once
go now to night
he have the rag regret of the lose hour of every passing moment
there be no thought of resistance now
yet with the instinctive fear of the irrevocable with the innate falseness of the human heart he want to keep open the way of retreat
he have never absent himself during the night
what do almayer know
what would almayer think
well ask he for the gun
moonlight night

colourable pretext
he would lie to almayer
what do it matter
he lie to himself every minute of his life
and for what
for woman
and such

everything get to be know even in this place
well he do not care
care for nothing but for the lost second
what if he should suddenly die
die before he see she
before he could
as with the sound of almayer 's laughter in his ear he urge his canoe in slanting course across the rapid current he try to tell himself that he could return at any moment
he would just go and look at the place where they use to meet at the tree under which he lie when she take his hand at the spot where she sit by his side
just go there and then return nothing more but when his little skiff touch the bank he leap out forget the painter and the canoe hang for moment amongst the bush and then swing out of sight before he have time to dash into the water and secure it
he be thunderstruck at first
now he could not go back unless he call up the rajah 's people to get boat and rower and the way to patalolo 's campong lead past aissa 's house
he go up the path with the eager eye and reluctant step of man pursue phantom and when he find himself at place where narrow track branch off to the left towards omar 's clearing he stand still with look of strained attention on his face as if listen to far off voice the voice of his fate
it be sound inarticulate but full of meaning and follow it there come rending and tear within his breast
he twist his finger together and the joint of his hand and arm crack
on his forehead the perspiration stand out in small pearly drop

he struggle with the sense of certain defeat lose his footing fall back into the darkness
with faint cry and an upward throw of his arm he give up as tired swimmer give up because the swamp craft be go from under his foot because the night be dark and the shore be far because death be well than strife
part ii chapter one
the light and heat fall upon the settlement the clearing and the river as if fling down by an angry hand
the land lie silent still and brilliant under the avalanche of burn ray that have destroy all sound and all motion have bury all shadow have choke every breath
no living thing dare to affront the serenity of this cloudless sky dare to revolt against the oppression of this glorious and cruel sunshine
strength and resolution body and mind alike be helpless and try to hide before the rush of the fire from heaven
only the frail butterfly the fearless child of the sun the capricious tyrant of the flower flutter audaciously in the open and their minute shadow hover in swarm over the droop blossom run lightly on the wither grass or glide on the dry and crack earth
no voice be hear in this hot noontide but the faint murmur of the river that hurry on in swirl and eddy
its sparkling wavelet chase each other in their joyous course to the shelter depth to the cool refuge of the sea

chapter two
sigh under the flame blue shiver of the sleep sea cool breath as if door have be swing upon the frozen space of the universe and with stir of leaf with the nod of bough with the tremble of slender branch the sea breeze strike the coast rush up the river sweep round the broad reach and travel on in soft ripple of darken water in the whisper of branch in the rustle of leaf of the awakened forest
it fan in lakamba 's campong the dull red of expire ember into pale brilliance and under its touch the slender upright spiral of smoke that rise from every glow heap sway waver and eddy down fill the twilight of clustered shade tree with the aromatic scent of the burn wood
the man who have be doze in the shade during the hot hour of the afternoon wake up and the silence of the big courtyard be break by the hesitate murmur of yet sleepy voice by cough and yawn with now and then burst of laughter loud hail name or joke send out in soft drawl
small group squat round the little fire and the monotonous undertone of talk fill the enclosure the talk of barbarian persistent steady repeat itself in the soft syllable in musical tone of the never end discourse of those man of the forest and the sea who can talk most of the day and all the night who never exhaust subject never seem able to thresh matter out to whom that talk be poetry and painting and music all art all history their only accomplishment their only superiority their only amusement
the talk of camp fire which speak of bravery and cunning of strange event and of far country of the news of yesterday and the news of to morrow
the talk about the dead and the living about those who fight and those who love
lakamba come out on the platform before his own house and sit down perspire half asleep and sulky in wooden armchair under the shade of the overhanging eave
through the darkness of the doorway he could hear the soft warbling of his womenkind busy round the loom where they be weave the checkered pattern of his gala sarong
right and leave of he on the flexible bamboo floor those of his follower to whom their distinguished birth long devotion or faithful service have give the privilege of use the chief 's house be sleep on mat or just sit up rub their eye while the more wakeful have muster enough energy to draw chessboard with red clay on fine mat and be now meditate silently over their move
above the prostrate form of the player who lie face downward support on elbow the sol of their foot wave irresolutely about in the absorbed meditation of the game there tower here and there the straight figure of an attentive spectator look down with dispassionate but profound interest
on the edge of the platform row of high heeled leather sandal stand range carefully in level line and against the rough wooden rail lean the slender shaft of the spear belong to these gentleman the broad blade of dull steel look very black in the redden light of approach sunset
boy of about twelve the personal attendant of lakamba squat at his master 's foot and hold up towards he silver siri box
slowly lakamba take the box open it and tear off piece of green leaf deposit in it pinch of lime morsel of gambier small bit of areca nut and wrap up the whole with dexterous twist
he pause morsel in hand seem to miss something turn his head from side to side slowly like man with stiff neck and ejaculate in an ill humour bass-- babalatchi
the player glance up quickly and look down again directly

there be movement of upturned face below by the fire and the cry trail over the enclosure in sing song tone
the thumping of wooden pestle husk the evening rice stop for moment and babalatchi 's name ring afresh shrilly on woman 's lip in various key

he be with the blind omar

babalatchi have go to see old omar late in the afternoon
the delicate manipulation of the ancient pirate 's susceptibility the skilful management of aissa 's violent impulse engross he to the exclusion of every other business interfere with his regular attendance upon his chief and protector even disturb his sleep for the last three night
that day when he leave his own bamboo hut which stand amongst other in lakamba 's campong his heart be heavy with anxiety and with doubt as to the success of his intrigue
he walk slowly with his usual air of detachment from his surrounding as if unaware that many sleepy eye watch from all part of the courtyard his progress towards small gate at its upper end
that gate give access to separate enclosure in which rather large house build of plank have be prepare by lakamba 's order for the reception of omar and aissa
it be superior kind of habitation which lakamba intend for the dwelling of his chief adviser whose ability be worth that honour he think
but after the consultation in the deserted clearing when babalatchi have disclose his plan they both have agree that the new house should be use at first to shelter omar and aissa after they have be persuade to leave the rajah 's place or have be kidnap from there as the case might be
babalatchi do not mind in the least the putting off of his own occupation of the house of honour because it have many advantage for the quiet work out of his plan
it have certain seclusion have an enclosure of its own and that enclosure communicate also with lakamba 's private courtyard at the back of his residence place set apart for the female household of the chief
the only communication with the river be through the great front courtyard always full of armed man and watchful eye
behind the whole group of building there stretch the level ground of rice clearing which in their turn be close in by the wall of untouched forest with undergrowth so thick and tangle that nothing but bullet and that fire at pretty close range could penetrate any distance there
babalatchi slip quietly through the little gate and close it tie up carefully the rattan fastening

his turban be half unroll and the end trail on the ground behind he
his jacket be open
he breathe thickly for moment before he speak
he come in bulangi 's boat he say and walk quietly till he be in my presence when the senseless fury of white man cause he to rush upon
have be in great danger go on the ambitious nobleman in an aggrieved tone
do you hear that babalatchi
that eater of swine aim blow at my face with his unclean fist
he try to rush amongst my household
six man be hold he now

can we hold he for ever

and now he say aloud release she and step back go fearless daughter go

they hear the renew tumult the girl 's clear voice call out let he go
then after pause in the din no long than half the human breath the name of aissa ring in shout loud discordant and piercing which send through they an involuntary shudder
old omar collapse on his carpet and moan feebly lakamba star with gloomy contempt in the direction of the inhuman sound but babalatchi force smile push his distinguished protector through the narrow gate in the stockade follow he and close it quickly
the old woman who have be most of the time kneel by the fire now rise glance round fearfully and crouch hiding behind the tree
the gate of the great courtyard fly open with great clatter before frantic kick and willems dart in carry aissa in his arm
he rush up the enclosure like tornado press the girl to his breast her arm round his neck her head hang back over his arm her eye close and her long hair nearly touch the ground
they appear for second in the glare of the fire then with immense stride he dash up the plank and disappear with his burden in the doorway of the big house
inside and outside the enclosure there be silence
omar lie support himself on his elbow his terrified face with its closed eye give he the appearance of man torment by nightmare
what be it
help to rise
he call out faintly
the old hag still crouch in the shadow star with bleared eye at the doorway of the big house and take no notice of his call
he listen for while then his arm give way and with deep sigh of discouragement he let himself fall on the carpet
the bough of the tree nod and tremble in the unsteady current of the light wind
leaf flutter down slowly from some high branch and rest on the ground immobile as if rest for ever in the glow of the fire but soon it stir then soar suddenly and fly spin and turn before the breath of the perfumed breeze drive helplessly into the dark night that have close over the land chapter three
for upwards of forty year abdulla have walk in the way of his lord
son of the rich syed selim bin sali the great mohammedan trader of the straits he go forth at the age of seventeen on his first commercial expedition as his father 's representative on board pilgrim ship charter by the wealthy arab to convey crowd of pious malays to the holy shrine
that be in the day when steam be not in those sea or at least not so much as now
the voyage be long and the young man 's eye be open to the wonder of many land
allah have make it his fate to become pilgrim very early in life
this be great favour of heaven and it could not have be bestow upon man who prize it more or who make himself more worthy of it by the unswerve piety of his heart and by the religious solemnity of his demeanour
later on it become clear that the book of his destiny contain the programme of wander life
he visit bombay and calcutta look in at the persian gulf behold in due course the high and barren coast of the gulf of suez and this be the limit of his wandering westward
he be then twenty seven and the writing on his forehead decree that the time have come for he to return to the straits and take from his die father 's hand the many thread of business that be spread over all the archipelago from sumatra to new guinea from batavia to palawan
very soon his ability his will strong to obstinacy his wisdom beyond his year cause he to be recognize as the head of family whose member and connection be find in every part of those sea
an uncle here brother there father in law in batavia another in palembang husband of numerous sister cousin innumerable scatter north south east and west in every place where there be trade the great family lie like network over the island
they lend money to prince influence the council room face if need be with peaceful intrepidity the white ruler who hold the land and the sea under the edge of sharp sword and they all pay great deference to abdulla listen to his advice enter into his plan because he be wise pious and fortunate
he bear himself with the humility become believer who never forget even for one moment of his wake life that he be the servant of the most high
he be largely charitable because the charitable man be the friend of allah and when he walk out of his house build of stone just outside the town of penang on his way to his godown in the port he have often to snatch his hand away sharply from under the lip of man of his race and creed and often he have to murmur deprecate word or even to rebuke with severity those who attempt to touch his knee with their finger tip in gratitude or supplication
he be very handsome and carry his small head high with meek gravity
his lofty brow straight nose narrow dark face with its chisel delicacy of feature give he an aristocratic appearance which proclaim his pure descent
his beard be trim close and to rounded point
his large brown eye look out steadily with sweetness that be belie by the expression of his thin lipped mouth
his aspect be serene
he have belief in his own prosperity which nothing could shake
restless like all his people he very seldom dwell for many day together in his splendid house in penang
owner of ship he be often on board one or another of they traverse in all direction the field of his operation
in every port he have household his own or that of relation to hail his advent with demonstrative joy
in every port there be rich and influential man eager to see he there be business to talk over there be important letter to read an immense correspondence enclose in silk envelope correspondence which have nothing to do with the infidel of colonial post office but come into his hand by devious yet safe way
it be leave for he by taciturn nakhodas of native trading craft or be deliver with profound salaam by travel stain and weary man who would withdraw from his presence call upon allah to bless the generous giver of splendid reward
and the news be always good and all his attempt always succeed and in his ear there ring always chorus of admiration of gratitude of humble entreaty
fortunate man
and his felicity be so complete that the good genius who order the star at his birth have not neglect by refinement of benevolence strange in such primitive being to provide he with desire difficult to attain and with an enemy hard to overcome
the envy of lingard 's political and commercial success and the wish to get the good of he in every way become abdulla 's mania the paramount interest of his life the salt of his existence
for the last few month he have be receive mysterious message from sambir urge he to decisive action
he have find the river couple of year ago and have be anchor more than once off that estuary where the till then rapid pantai spread slowly over the lowland seem to hesitate before it flow gently through twenty outlet over maze of mudflat sandbank and reef into the expectant sea
he have never attempt the entrance however because man of his race although brave and adventurous traveller lack the true seamanlike instinct and he be afraid of getting wreck
he could not bear the idea of the rajah laut be able to boast that abdulla bin selim like other and less man have also come to grief when try to wrest his secret from he
meantime he return encourage answer to his unknown friend in sambir and wait for his opportunity in the calm certitude of ultimate triumph
such be the man whom lakamba and babalatchi expect to see for the first time on the night of willems return to aissa
babalatchi who have be torment for three day by the fear of have over reach himself in his little plot now feel sure of his white man feel lighthearted and happy as he superintend the preparation in the courtyard for abdulla 's reception
half way between lakamba 's house and the river pile of dry wood be make ready for the torch that would set fire to it at the moment of abdulla 's landing
between this and the house again there be range in semicircle set of low bamboo frame and on those be pile all the carpet and cushion of lakamba 's household
it have be decide that the reception be to take place in the open air and that it should be make impressive by the great number of lakamba 's retainer who clothe in clean white with their red sarong gather round their waist chopper at side and lance in hand be move about the compound or gather into small knot discuss eagerly the come ceremony
two little fire burn brightly on the water 's edge on each side of the landing place
small heap of damar gum torch lie by each and between they babalatchi stroll backwards and forwards stop often with his face to the river and his head on one side listen to the sound that come from the darkness over the water
there be no moon and the night be very clear overhead but after the afternoon breeze have expire in fitful puff the vapour hang thickening over the glance surface of the pantai and cling to the shore hide from view the middle of the stream
cry in the mist then another and before babalatchi could answer two little canoe dash up to the landing place and two of the principal citizen of sambir daoud sahamin and hamet bahassoen who have be confidentially invite to meet abdulla land quickly and after greet babalatchi walk up the dark courtyard towards the house
the little stir cause by their arrival soon subside and another silent hour drag its slow length while babalatchi tramp up and down between the fire his face grow more anxious with every passing moment
at last there be hear loud hail from down the river
at call from babalatchi man run down to the riverside and snatch the torch thrust they into the fire then wave they above their head till they burst into flame
the smoke ascend in thick wispy stream and hang in ruddy cloud above the glare that light up the courtyard and flash over the water show three long canoe man by many paddler lie little off the man in they lift their paddle on high and dip they down together in an easy stroke that keep the small flotilla motionless in the strong current exactly abreast of the landing place

chapter four

nothing but misfortune she whisper absently to herself
nothing but misfortune to we who be not white
the anger and excitement die out of her face and she look straight at willems with an intense and mournful gaze
he recover his sens and his power of speech with sudden start
aissa he exclaim and the word break out through his lip with hurried nervousness

believe in

very far you and
he do not stop to ask himself whether he could escape and how and where
he be carry away by the flood of hate disgust and contempt of white man for that blood which be not his blood for that race which be not his race for the brown skin for the heart false like the sea black than night
this feeling of repulsion overmaster his reason in clear conviction of the impossibility for he to live with her people
he urge she passionately to fly with he because out of all that abhor crowd he want this one woman but want she away from they away from that race of slave and cut throat from which she spring
he want she for himself far from everybody in some safe and dumb solitude
and as he speak his anger and contempt rise his hate become almost fear and his desire of she grow immense burning illogical and merciless cry to he through all his sens louder than his hate strong than his fear deep than his contempt irresistible and certain like death itself
stand at little distance just within the light but on the threshold of that darkness from which she have come she listen one hand still behind she back the other arm stretch out with the hand half open as if to catch the fleeting word that ring around she passionate menacing imploring but all ting with the anguish of his suffering all hurry by the impatience that gnaw his breast
and while she listen she feel slowing down of her heart beat as the meaning of his appeal grow clear before her indignant eye as she see with rage and pain the edifice of her love her own work crumble slowly to piece destroy by that man 's fear by that man 's falseness
her memory recall the day by the brook when she have listen to other word to other thought to promise and to pleading for other thing which come from that man 's lip at the bidding of her look or her smile at the nod of her head at the whisper of her lip
be there then in his heart something else than her image other desire than the desire of her love other fear than the fear of lose she
how could that be have she grow ugly or old in moment
she be appal surprised and angry with the anger of unexpected humiliation and her eye look fixedly sombre and steady at that man bear in the land of violence and of evil wherefrom nothing but misfortune come to those who be not white
instead of thinking of her caress instead of forget all the world in her embrace he be think yet of his people of that people that steal every land master every sea that know no mercy and no truth know nothing but its own strength
man of strong arm and of false heart

he be mad mad with fear but he should not escape she

he follow her step by step till at last they both stop face each other under the big tree of the enclosure
the solitary exile of the forest great motionless and solemn in his abandonment leave alone by the life of age that have be push away from he by those pigmy that creep at his foot tower high and straight above their head
he seem to look on dispassionate and impose in his lonely greatness spread his branch wide in gesture of lofty protection as if to hide they in the sombre shelter of innumerable leaf as if move by the disdainful compassion of the strong by the scornful pity of an aged giant to screen this struggle of two human heart from the cold scrutiny of glittering star
the last cry of his appeal to her mercy rise loud vibrate under the sombre canopy dart among the bough startle the white bird that sleep wing to wing and die without an echo strangle in the dense mass of unstirre leaf

he fight without sound striking futile blow dash from side to side obstinate hopeless and always beat back like man bewitch within the invisible sweep of magic circle

chapter four consciously or unconsciously man be proud of their firmness steadfastness of purpose directness of aim
they go straight towards their desire to the accomplishment of virtue sometimes of crime in an uplifting persuasion of their firmness
they walk the road of life the road fence in by their taste prejudice disdain or enthusiasm generally honest invariably stupid and be proud of never lose their way
if they do stop it be to look for moment over the hedge that make they safe to look at the misty valley at the distant peak at cliff and morass at the dark forest and the hazy plain where other human being grope their day painfully away stumble over the bone of the wise over the unburied remain of their predecessor who die alone in gloom or in sunshine halfway from anywhere
the man of purpose do not understand and go on full of contempt
he never lose his way
he know where he be go and what he want
travel on he achieve great length without any breadth and batter besmirch and weary he touch the goal at last he grasp the reward of his perseverance of his virtue of his healthy optimism an untruthful tombstone over dark and soon forget grave
lingard have never hesitate in his life
why should he
he have be most successful trader and man lucky in his fight skilful in navigation undeniably first in seamanship in those sea
he know it
have he not hear the voice of common consent
the voice of the world that respect he so much the whole world to he for to we the limit of the universe be strictly define by those we know
there be nothing for we outside the babble of praise and blame on familiar lip and beyond our last acquaintance there lie only vast chaos chaos of laughter and tear which concern we not laughter and tear unpleasant wicked morbid contemptible because hear imperfectly by ear rebellious to strange sound
to lingard simple himself all thing be simple
he seldom read
book be not much in his way and he have to work hard navigate trading and also in obedience to his benevolent instinct shape stray life he find here and there under his busy hand
he remember the sunday school teaching of his native village and the discourse of the black coat gentleman connect with the mission to fishermen and seamen whose yawl rig boat dart through rain squall amongst the coaster wind bind in falmouth bay be part of those precious picture of his youthful day that linger in his memory
as clever sky pilot as you could wish to see he would say with conviction and the good man to handle boat in any weather ever do meet
such be the agency that have roughly shape his young soul before he go away to see the world in southern go ship before he go ignorant and happy heavy of hand pure in heart profane in speech to give himself up to the great sea that take his life and give he his fortune
when think of his rise in the world commander of ship then shipowner then man of much capital respect wherever he go lingard in word the rajah laut he be amazed and awed by his fate that seem to his ill inform mind the most wondrous know in the annal of man
his experience appear to he immense and conclusive teach he the lesson of the simplicity of life
in life as in seamanship there be only two way of do thing the right way and the wrong way
common sense and experience teach man the way that be right
the other be for lubber and fool and lead in seamanship to loss of spar and sail or shipwreck in life to loss of money and consideration or to an unlucky knock on the head
he do not consider it his duty to be angry with rascal
he be only angry with thing he could not understand but for the weakness of humanity he could find contemptuous tolerance
it be manifest that he be wise and lucky otherwise how could he have be as successful in life as he have been?--he have an inclination to set right the life of other people just as he could hardly refrain in defiance of nautical etiquette from interfere with his chief officer when the crew be send up new topmast or generally when busy about what he call heavy job
he be meddlesome with perfect modesty if he know thing or two there be no merit in it
hard knock teach wisdom my boy he use to say and you have well take the advice of man who have be fool in his time
have another
and my boy as rule take the cool drink the advice and the consequent help which lingard feel himself bind in honour to give so as to back up his opinion like an honest man
captain tom go sail from island to island appear unexpectedly in various locality beam noisy anecdotal commendatory or comminatory but always welcome
it be only since his return to sambir that the old seaman have for the first time know doubt and unhappiness the loss of the flash plant firmly and for ever on ledge of rock at the north end of gaspar straits in the uncertain light of cloudy morning shake he considerably and the amazing news which he hear on his arrival in sambir be not make to soothe his feeling
good many year ago prompt by his love of adventure he with infinite trouble have find out and survey for his own benefit only the entrance to that river where he have hear through native report new settlement of malays be form
no doubt he think at the time mostly of personal gain but receive with hearty friendliness by patalolo he soon come to like the ruler and the people offer his counsel and his help and know nothing of arcadia he dream of arcadian happiness for that little corner of the world which he love to think all his own
his deep seated and immovable conviction that only he he lingard know what be good for they be characteristic of he and after all not so very far wrong
he would make they happy whether or no he say and he mean it
his trade bring prosperity to the young state and the fear of his heavy hand secure its internal peace for many year
he look proudly upon his work
with every pass year he love more the land the people the muddy river that if he could help it would carry no other craft but the flash on its unclean and friendly surface
as he slowly warp his vessel up stream he would scan with know look the riverside clearing and pronounce solemn judgment upon the prospect of the season 's rice crop
he know every settler on the bank between the sea and sambir he know their wife their child he know every individual of the multi coloured group that stand on the flimsy platform of tiny reed dwelling build over the water wave their hand and shout shrilly kapal layer
while the flash sweep slowly through the populated reach to enter the lonely stretch of sparkling brown water border by the dense and silent forest whose big tree nod their outspread bough gently in the faint warm breeze as if in sign of tender but melancholy welcome
he love it all the landscape of brown gold and brilliant emerald under the dome of hot sapphire the whisper big tree the loquacious nipa palm that rattle their leaf volubly in the night breeze as if in haste to tell he all the secret of the great forest behind they
he love the heavy scent of blossom and black earth that breath of life and of death which linger over his brig in the damp air of tepid and peaceful night
he love the narrow and sombre creek stranger to sunshine black smooth tortuous like byway of despair
he like even the troop of sorrowful face monkey that profane the quiet spot with capricious gambol and insane gesture of inhuman madness
he love everything there animate or inanimate the very mud of the riverside the very alligator enormous and stolid bask on it with impertinent unconcern
their size be source of pride to he
