part iv chapter one
the night be very dark
for the first time in many month the east coast sleep unseen by the star under veil of motionless cloud that drive before the first breath of the rainy monsoon have drift slowly from the eastward all the afternoon pursue the decline sun with its mass of black and grey that seem to chase the light with wicked intent and with an ominous and gloomy steadiness as though conscious of the message of violence and turmoil they carry
at the sun 's disappearance below the western horizon the immense cloud in quicken motion grapple with the glow of retreat light and roll down to the clear and jagged outline of the distant mountain hang arrest above the steaming forest hang low silent and menacing over the unstirre tree top withhold the blessing of rain nurse the wrath of its thunder undecided as if brood over its own power for good or for evil
babalatchi come out of the red and smoky light of his little bamboo house glance upwards draw in long breath of the warm and stagnant air and stand for moment with his good eye close tightly as if intimidate by the unwonted and deep silence of lakamba 's courtyard
when he open his eye he have recover his sight so far that he could distinguish the various degree of formless blackness which mark the place of tree of abandon house of riverside bush on the dark background of the night
the careworn sage walk cautiously down the deserted courtyard to the waterside and stand on the bank listen to the voice of the invisible river that flow at his foot listen to the soft whisper to the deep murmur to the sudden gurgle and the short hiss of the swift current racing along the bank through the hot darkness
he stand with his face turn to the river and it seem to he that he could breathe easy with the knowledge of the clear vast space before he then after while he lean heavily forward on his staff his chin fall on his breast and deep sigh be his answer to the selfish discourse of the river that hurry on unceasing and fast regardless of joy or sorrow of suffering and of strife of failure and triumph that live on its bank
the brown water be there ready to carry friend or enemy to nurse love or hate on its submissive and heartless bosom to help or to hinder to save life or give death the great and rapid river deliverance prison refuge or grave
perchance such thought as these cause babalatchi to send another mournful sigh into the trail mist of the unconcerned pantai
the barbarous politician have forget the recent success of his plotting in the melancholy contemplation of sorrow that make the night black the clammy heat more oppressive the still air more heavy the dumb solitude more significant of torment than of peace
he have spend the night before by the side of the die omar and now after twenty four hour his memory persist in return to that low and sombre reed hut from which the fierce spirit of the incomparably accomplished pirate take its flight to learn too late in bad world the error of its earthly way
the mind of the savage statesman chasten by bereavement feel for moment the weight of his loneliness with keen perception worthy even of sensibility exasperate by all the refinement of tender sentiment that glorious civilization bring in its train among other blessing and virtue into this excellent world
for the space of about thirty second half naked betel chew pessimist stand upon the bank of the tropical river on the edge of the still and immense forest man angry powerless empty handed with cry of bitter discontent ready on his lip cry that have it come out would have ring through the virgin solitude of the wood as true as great as profound as any philosophical shriek that ever come from the depth of an easy chair to disturb the impure wilderness of chimney and roof
for half minute and no more do babalatchi face the god in the sublime privilege of his revolt and then the one eyed puller of wire become himself again full of care and wisdom and far reach plan and victim to the torment superstition of his race
the night no matter how quiet be never perfectly silent to attentive ear and now babalatchi fancy he could detect in it other noise than those cause by the ripple and eddy of the river
he turn his head sharply to the right and to the left in succession and then spin round quickly in startled and watchful manner as if he have expect to see the blind ghost of his depart leader wander in the obscurity of the empty courtyard behind his back
nothing there

no doubt ghostly voice of complaining and angry spirit

the elastic bamboo floor of the hut bend under the heavy weight of the old seaman who stand within the threshold try to look into the smoky gloom of the low dwelling
under the torch thrust into the cleft of stick fasten at right angle to the middle stay of the ridge pole lie red patch of light show few shabby mat and corner of big wooden chest the rest of which be lose in shadow
in the obscurity of the more remote part of the house lance head brass tray hang on the wall the long barrel of gun lean against the chest catch the stray ray of the smoky illumination in trembling gleam that waver disappear reappear go out come back as if engage in doubtful struggle with the darkness that lie in wait in distant corner seem to dart out viciously towards its feeble enemy
the vast space under the high pitch of the roof be fill with thick cloud of smoke whose under side level like ceiling reflect the light of the sway dull flame while at the top it ooze out through the imperfect thatch of dry palm leaf
an indescribable and complicated smell make up of the exhalation of damp earth below of the taint of dry fish and of the effluvia of rot vegetable matter pervade the place and cause lingard to sniff strongly as he stride over sit on the chest and lean his elbow on his knee take his head between his hand and star at the doorway thoughtfully
babalatchi move about in the shadow whisper to an indistinct form or two that flit about at the far end of the hut

justice only

justice only
it be his duty that justice should be do and by his own hand
he do not like to think how

it be inevitable and its time be near

how he have like the man his assurance his push his desire to get on his conceited good humour and his selfish eloquence
he have like his very fault those fault that have so many to he sympathetic side
and he have always deal fairly by he from the very beginning and he would deal fairly by he now to the very end
this last thought darken lingard 's feature with responsive and menacing frown
the doer of justice sit with compressed lip and heavy heart while in the calm darkness outside the silent world seem to be wait breathlessly for that justice he hold in his hand in his strong hand:--ready to strike reluctant to move

to the old adventurer the idea that fire arm could be dangerous in other hand than his own do not occur readily and certainly not in connection with willems
he be so busy with the thought about what he consider his own sacred duty that he could not give any consideration to the probable action of the man of whom he think as one may think of an execute criminal with wonder indignation temper by scornful pity

she seem however to realize directly the hopelessness or the difficulty of the undertaking stop hesitate totter back slowly then after blink dully fall suddenly on her knee amongst the white ash and bend over the heap of smoulder coal distend her sink cheek in steady effort to blow up the hide spark into useful blaze

the matter and the manner of her speech hold lingard motionless attentive and approve against his will

and be afraid

part chapter one almayer prop alone on the verandah of his house with both his elbow on the table and hold his head between his hand star before he away over the stretch of sprout young grass in his courtyard and over the short jetty with its cluster of small canoe amongst which his big whale boat float high like white mother of all that dark and aquatic brood

the sun be sink

abreast of the house the river be empty but for the motionless schooner
high up solitary log come out from the bend above and go on drift slowly down the straight reach dead and wander tree go out to its grave in the sea between two rank of tree motionless and live
and almayer sit his face in his hand look on and hat all this the muddy river the fade blue of the sky the black log pass by on its first and last voyage the green sea of leaf the sea that glow shimmer and stir above the uniform and impenetrable gloom of the forest the joyous sea of live green powder with the brilliant dust of oblique sunray

and yet all this be very precious to he

he find out very soon that trade in sambir mean something entirely different
he could not guide patalolo control the irrepressible old sahamin or restrain the youthful vagary of the fierce bahassoen with pen ink and paper

through the half open shutter ray of sunlight ray merciless and crude come into the room beat in the early morning upon the safe in the far off corner then travel against the sun cut at midday the big desk in two with its solid and clean edge brilliance with its hot brilliance in which swarm of fly hover in dance flight over some dirty plate forget there amongst yellow paper for many day

keats author be note
the main characteristic of this volume consist in this that all the story compose it belong not only to the same period but have be write one after another in the order in which they appear in the book
the period be that which follow on my connection with blackwood 's magazine
have just finish write the end of the tether and be cast about for some subject which could be develop in short form than the tale in the volume of youth when the instance of steamship full of return cooly from singapore to some port in northern china occur to my recollection
year before have hear it be talk about in the east as recent occurrence
it be for we merely one subject of conversation amongst many other of the kind
man earn their bread in any very specialized occupation will talk shop not only because it be the most vital interest of their life but also because they have not much knowledge of other subject
they have never have the time to get acquaint with they
life for most of we be not so much hard as an exacting taskmaster
never meet anybody personally concerned in this affair the interest of which for we be of course not the bad weather but the extraordinary complication bring into the ship 's life at moment of exceptional stress by the human element below her deck
neither be the story itself ever enlarge upon in my hearing
in that company each of we could imagine easily what the whole thing be like
the financial difficulty of it present also human problem be solve by mind much too simple to be perplex by anything in the world except man 's idle talk for which it be not adapt
from the first the mere anecdote the mere statement might say that such thing have happen on the high sea appear to sufficient subject for meditation
yet it be but bit of sea yarn after all
feel that to bring out its deep significance which be quite apparent to something other something more be require lead motive that would harmonize all these violent noise and point of view that would put all that elemental fury into its proper place
what be need of course be captain macwhirr
directly perceive he could see that he be the man for the situation
do not mean to say that ever see captain macwhirr in the flesh or have ever come in contact with his literal mind and his dauntless temperament
macwhirr be not an acquaintance of few hour or few week or few month
he be the product of twenty year of life
my own life
conscious invention have little to do with he
if it be true that captain macwhirr never walk and breathe on this earth which find for my part extremely difficult to believe can also assure my reader that he be perfectly authentic
may venture to assert the same of every aspect of the story while confess that the particular typhoon of the tale be not typhoon of my actual experience
at its first appearance typhoon the story be class by some critic as deliberately intend storm piece
other pick out macwhirr in whom they perceive definite symbolic intention
neither be exclusively my intention
both the typhoon and captain macwhirr present themselves to as the necessity of the deep conviction with which approach the subject of the story
it be their opportunity
it be also my opportunity and it would be vain to discourse about what make of it in handful of page since the page themselves be here between the cover of this volume to speak for themselves
this be belated reflection
if it have occur to before it would have perhaps do away with the existence of this author 's note for indeed the same remark apply to every story in this volume
none of they be story of experience in the absolute sense of the word
experience in they be but the canvas of the attempt picture
each of they have its more than one intention
with each the question be what the writer have do with his opportunity and each answer the question for itself in word which if may say so without undue solemnity be write with conscientious regard for the truth of my own sensation
and each of those story to mean something must justify itself in its own way to the conscience of each successive reader
falk”--the second story in the volume offend the delicacy of one critic at least by certain peculiarity of its subject
but what be the subject of falk
personally do not feel so very certain about it
he who read must find out for himself
my intention in write falk be not to shock anybody
as in most of my writing insist not on the event but on their effect upon the person in the tale
but in everything have write there be always one invariable intention and that be to capture the reader 's attention by secure his interest and enlist his sympathy for the matter in hand whatever it may be within the limit of the visible world and within the boundary of human emotion
may safely say that falk be absolutely true to my experience of certain straightforward character combine perfectly natural ruthlessness with certain amount of moral delicacy
falk obey the law of self preservation without the slight misgiving as to his right but at crucial turn of that ruthlessly preserve life he will not condescend to dodge the truth
as he be present as sensitive enough to be affect permanently by certain unusual experience that experience have to be set by before the reader vividly but it be not the subject of the tale
if we go by mere fact then the subject be falk 's attempt to get marry in which the narrator of the tale find himself unexpectedly involve both on its ruthless and its delicate side
falk share with one other of my story the return in the tales of unrest volume the distinction of never having be serialize
think the copy be show to the editor of some magazine who reject it indignantly on the sole ground that the girl never say anything
this be perfectly true
from first to last hermann 's niece utter no word in the tale and it be not because she be dumb but for the simple reason that whenever she happen to come under the observation of the narrator she have either no occasion or be too profoundly move to speak
the editor who obviously have read the story might have perceive that for himself
apparently he do not and refrain from point out the impossibility to he because since he do not venture to say that the girl do not live feel no concern at his indignation
all the other story be serialize
the typhoon appear in the early number of the pall mall magazine then under the direction of the late mr. halkett
it be on that occasion too that see for the first time my conception render by an artist in another medium
mr. maurice grieffenhagen know how to combine in his illustration the effect of his own most distinguished personal vision with an absolute fidelity to the inspiration of the writer
amy foster be publish in the illustrated london news with fine drawing of amy on her day out give tea to the child at her home in hat with big feather
to morrow appear first in the pall mall magazine
of that story will only say that it strike many people by its adaptability to the stage and that be induce to dramatize it under the title of one day more up to the present my only effort in that direction
may also add that each of the four story on their appearance in book form be pick out on various ground as the good of the lot by different critic who review the volume with warmth of appreciation and understanding sympathetic insight and friendliness of expression for which can not be sufficiently grateful
j. c. typhoon captain macwhirr of the steamer nan shan have physiognomy that in the order of material appearance be the exact counterpart of his mind it present no mark characteristic of firmness or stupidity it have no pronounce characteristic whatever it be simply ordinary irresponsive and unruffled
the only thing his aspect might have be say to suggest at time be bashfulness because he would sit in business office ashore sunburnt and smile faintly with downcast eye
when he raise they they be perceive to be direct in their glance and of blue colour
his hair be fair and extremely fine clasp from temple to temple the bald dome of his skull in clamp as of fluffy silk
the hair of his face on the contrary carroty and flaming resemble growth of copper wire clip short to the line of the lip while no matter how close he shave fiery metallic gleams pass when he move his head over the surface of his cheek
he be rather below the medium height bit round shoulder and so sturdy of limb that his clothe always look shade too tight for his arm and leg
as if unable to grasp what be due to the difference of latitude he wear brown bowler hat complete suit of brownish hue and clumsy black boot
these harbour togs give to his thick figure an air of stiff and uncouth smartness
thin silver watch chain loop his waistcoat and he never leave his ship for the shore without clutch in his powerful hairy fist an elegant umbrella of the very good quality but generally unroll
young jukes the chief mate attend his commander to the gangway would sometimes venture to say with the great gentleness allow sir”--and possess himself of the umbrella deferentially would elevate the ferule shake the fold twirl neat furl in jiffy and hand it back go through the performance with face of such portentous gravity that mr. solomon rout the chief engineer smoke his morning cigar over the skylight would turn away his head in order to hide smile
oh aye
the blessed gamp
thank ee jukes thank ee would mutter captain macwhirr heartily without look up
have just enough imagination to carry he through each successive day and no more he be tranquilly sure of himself and from the very same cause he be not in the least conceited
it be your imaginative superior who be touchy overbearing and difficult to please but every ship captain macwhirr command be the float abode of harmony and peace
it be in truth as impossible for he to take flight of fancy as it would be for watchmaker to put together chronometer with nothing except two pound hammer and whip saw in the way of tool
yet the uninteresting life of man so entirely give to the actuality of the bare existence have their mysterious side
it be impossible in captain macwhirr 's case for instance to understand what under heaven could have induce that perfectly satisfactory son of petty grocer in belfast to run away to sea
and yet he have do that very thing at the age of fifteen
it be enough when you think it over to give you the idea of an immense potent and invisible hand thrust into the ant heap of the earth lay hold of shoulder knock head together and set the unconscious face of the multitude towards inconceivable goal and in undreamt of direction
his father never really forgave he for this undutiful stupidity
we could have get on without he he use to say later on but there be the business
and he an only son too
his mother weep very much after his disappearance
as it have never occur to he to leave word behind he be mourn over for dead till after eight month his first letter arrive from talcahuano
it be short and contain the statement we have very fine weather on our passage out
but evidently in the writer 's mind the only important intelligence be to the effect that his captain have on the very day of writing enter he regularly on the ship 's article as ordinary seaman
because can do the work he explain
the mother again weep copiously while the remark tom 's an ass express the emotion of the father
he be corpulent man with gift for sly chaffing which to the end of his life he exercise in his intercourse with his son little pityingly as if upon half witted person
macwhirr 's visit to his home be necessarily rare and in the course of year he despatch other letter to his parent inform they of his successive promotion and of his movement upon the vast earth
in these missive could be find sentence like this the heat here be very great
or on christmas day at p. m. we fall in with some iceberg
the old people ultimately become acquaint with good many name of ship and with the name of the skipper who command they with the name of scots and english shipowner with the name of sea ocean strait promontory with outlandish name of lumber port of rice port of cotton port with the name of island with the name of their son 's young woman
she be call lucy
it do not suggest itself to he to mention whether he think the name pretty
and then they die

all these event have take place many year before the morning when in the chart room of the steamer nan shan he stand confront by the fall of barometer he have no reason to distrust
the fall take into account the excellence of the instrument the time of the year and the ship 's position on the terrestrial globe be of nature ominously prophetic but the red face of the man betray no sort of inward disturbance
omen be as nothing to he and he be unable to discover the message of prophecy till the fulfilment have bring it home to his very door
that be fall and no mistake he think

the nan shan be on her way from the southward to the treaty port of fu chau with some cargo in her low hold and two hundred chinese cooly return to their village home in the province of fo kien after few year of work in various tropical colony
the morning be fine the oily sea heave without sparkle and there be queer white misty patch in the sky like halo of the sun
the fore deck pack with chinamen be full of sombre clothing yellow face and pigtail sprinkle over with good many naked shoulder for there be no wind and the heat be close
the cooly lounge talk smoke or star over the rail some draw water over the side sluice each other few sleep on hatch while several small party of six sit on their heel surround iron tray with plate of rice and tiny teacup and every single celestial of they be carry with he all he have in the world wooden chest with ring lock and brass on the corner contain the saving of his labour some clothe of ceremony stick of incense little opium maybe bit of nameless rubbish of conventional value and small hoard of silver dollar toil for in coal lighter win in gambling house or in petty trading grub out of earth sweat out in mine on railway line in deadly jungle under heavy burden amass patiently guard with care cherish fiercely
cross swell have set in from the direction of formosa channel about ten o'clock without disturb these passenger much because the nan shan with her flat bottom roll chock on bilge and great breadth of beam have the reputation of an exceptionally steady ship in sea way

captain macwhirr meantime have go on the bridge and into the chart room where letter commence two day before await termination
these long letter begin with the word my darling wife and the steward between the scrubbing of the floor and the dusting of chronometer box snatch at every opportunity to read they
they interest he much more than they possibly could the woman for whose eye they be intend and this for the reason that they relate in minute detail each successive trip of the nan shan her master faithful to fact which alone his consciousness reflect would set they down with painstaking care upon many page
the house in northern suburb to which these page be address have bit of garden before the bow window deep porch of good appearance coloured glass with imitation lead frame in the front door
he pay five and forty pound year for it and do not think the rent too high because mrs. macwhirr pretentious person with scraggy neck and disdainful manner be admittedly ladylike and in the neighbourhood consider as quite superior
the only secret of her life be her abject terror of the time when her husband would come home to stay for good
under the same roof there dwell also daughter call lydia and son tom
these two be but slightly acquaint with their father
mainly they know he as rare but privileged visitor who of an evening smoke his pipe in the dining room and sleep in the house
the lanky girl upon the whole be rather ashamed of he the boy be frankly and utterly indifferent in straightforward delightful unaffected way manly boy have
and captain macwhirr write home from the coast of china twelve time every year desire quaintly to be remember to the child and subscribe himself your love husband as calmly as if the word so long use by so many man be apart from their shape wear out thing and of fade meaning
the china seas north and south be narrow sea
they be sea full of every day eloquent fact such as island sand bank reef swift and changeable current tangle fact that nevertheless speak to seaman in clear and definite language
their speech appeal to captain macwhirr 's sense of reality so forcibly that he have give up his state room below and practically live all his day on the bridge of his ship often have his meal send up and sleep at night in the chart room
and he indite there his home letter
each of they without exception contain the phrase the weather have be very fine this trip or some other form of statement to that effect
and this statement too in its wonderful persistence be of the same perfect accuracy as all the other they contain
mr. rout likewise write letter only no one on board know how chatty he could be pen in hand because the chief engineer have enough imagination to keep his desk lock
his wife relish his style greatly
